meta_description: The Council of Scientific & Industrial Research (CSIR) in collaboration with the University Grants Commission (UGC) is set to release the notification for
content:
### CSIR UGC NET 2026 June Cycle Notification & Online Application

The National Testing Agency (NTA), on behalf of the Council of Scientific & Industrial Research (CSIR) and University Grants Commission (UGC), will soon release the official notification for the CSIR UGC NET June 2026 examination. This national-level eligibility test determines the eligibility of Indian nationals for Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) and for Lectureship (LS)/Assistant Professorship in Indian Universities and Colleges. Aspiring candidates in Science and Technology streams are advised to keep an eye on official updates.

#### Important Dates & Schedules

Candidates planning to apply for the CSIR UGC NET June 2026 cycle should note the tentative schedule provided below. Official dates will be announced with the detailed notification.

| Event                         | Date (Tentative for June 2026 Cycle) |
| :---------------------------- | :---------------------------------- |
| Online Application Start Date | To be announced                     |
| Last Date for Online Application | To be announced                     |
| Last Date for Fee Payment     | To be announced                     |
| Application Correction Window | To be announced                     |
| Admit Card Release Date       | To be announced                     |
| **Examination Date**          | **To be announced (Expected June 2026)** |
| Result Declaration            | To be announced                     |

#### Application Fees

The application fee for the CSIR UGC NET examination typically varies based on the candidate's category. The payment must be made online through various modes like Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking. Please refer to the official notification for exact figures.

| Category                      | Application Fee (Expected) |
| :---------------------------- | :------------------------- |
| General/EWS                   | ₹1100/-                    |
| OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)        | ₹550/-                     |
| SC/ST/PwD                     | ₹275/-                     |
| Third Gender                  | ₹275/-                     |

*Note: Application fees are non-refundable. Please check the official notification for any changes in fee structure.*

#### Eligibility Criteria

Aspirants must meet the prescribed eligibility criteria to apply for the CSIR UGC NET June 2026 examination.

**1. Educational Qualification:**
*   **M.Sc. or equivalent degree/Integrated BS-MS/BS-4 years/BE/B.Tech/B.Pharma/MBBS** with at least 55% marks for General (UR)/General-EWS and OBC candidates.
*   **50% marks** for SC/ST/Third Gender, Persons with Disability (PwD) candidates.
*   Candidates enrolled for M.Sc. or possessing a 10+2+3 year's of education and are in their final year/semester of M.Sc. or equivalent degree, or whose result is awaited, are also eligible to apply. Such candidates will be admitted provisionally upon submitting required documents within a stipulated timeframe.

**2. Age Limit (as on the specified cut-off date in the official notification):**
*   **For Junior Research Fellowship (JRF):** Maximum 28 years.
    *   Age relaxation of up to 5 years is provided for SC/ST/Third Gender/PwD/Female applicants.
    *   Age relaxation of up to 3 years is provided for OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) applicants.
*   **For Lectureship (LS)/Assistant Professorship:** No upper age limit.

#### How to Apply

Follow these step-by-step instructions to apply online for the CSIR UGC NET June 2026 examination:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Navigate to the official NTA CSIR NET website (typically `csirnet.nta.ac.in`).
2.  **New Registration:** Click on the "New Registration" or "Apply Online" link to begin the process.
3.  **Fill Registration Form:** Provide basic details such as your name, parent's name, valid mobile number, and email ID to generate a unique application number and password.
4.  **Complete Application Form:** Log in with your generated application number and password. Carefully fill in all personal details, educational qualifications, preferred exam centers, and other required information.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned images of your recent passport-sized photograph and signature in the prescribed format and dimensions as specified in the notification.
6.  **Pay Application Fee:** Proceed to pay the application fee online using available options such as Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking.
7.  **Review and Submit:** Thoroughly review all the entered details to ensure accuracy before final submission. Once satisfied, click on the "Submit" button.
8.  **Print Confirmation Page:** After successful submission and payment, a confirmation page will be generated. Download and print this page for your records and future reference.

Candidates are strongly advised to regularly check the official website for the latest updates and detailed information regarding the CSIR UGC NET June 2026 notification.

| Education wise Category       | Total Vacancies (Approx.) |
| :---------------------------- | :------------------------ |
| 10th Pass 12th Pass Govt Jobs | 10,000+                   |
| Govt Jobs for Graduates       | 10,000+                   |
| Govt Jobs for Engineers       | 1,000+                    |
| Govt Jobs for Diploma         | 1,000+                    |
| Govt Jobs for ITI             | 5,000+                    |
| Govt Jobs for Teachers        | 10,000+                   |
| Govt Jobs for Assistants      | 10,000+                   |
| Govt Jobs for Clerk           | 5,000+                    |
| Govt Jobs for MBA             | 100+                      |
| Govt Jobs for MCA             | 500+                      |
| Govt Jobs for Law             | 100+                      |

### Application Fee

Application fees vary widely depending on the specific recruitment and the recruiting organization. Candidates are advised to consult the individual official job notifications for exact fee structures, payment methods, and any applicable fee relaxations for reserved categories. Generally, fees can range from nil to several hundred rupees.

### How to Access Employment News & Apply

1.  **Review Highlights:** Browse the weekly job highlights provided on this page to identify recruitments of interest.
2.  **Access Official Employment News:** For detailed information and official notifications, visit the official Employment News website at `www.employmentnews.gov.in` or its e-version platform at `e-employmentnews.co.in`.
3.  **Subscribe to E-Paper (Optional):** You can subscribe to the weekly e-version of Employment News online for regular updates directly to your inbox. Subscription costs are generally ₹400 for 1 year, ₹750 for 2 years, and ₹1050 for 3 years.
4.  **Refer to Specific Notifications:** Each job highlight corresponds to a detailed recruitment advertisement. Candidates must navigate to the official website of the respective recruiting body (e.g., UPSC, SBI, Indian Army, AIIMS) to find the comprehensive notification, eligibility criteria, application fees, age limits, and step-by-step application instructions.
5.  **Online Application:** Most government job applications are submitted online. Follow the 'Apply Online' links provided in the detailed notifications on the official recruitment portals.

### Important Schedules & Timelines

As the Employment News is a weekly compilation of various job alerts, the start dates, end dates, and exam dates are unique to each recruitment. The tables above provide the 'Last Date' for applications for the jobs highlighted in each respective week. For precise schedules including application opening dates, exam dates, and result announcements for any specific post, candidates **must refer to the full official notification** published by the concerned recruiting authority.

### FAQs about Employment News Paper

**Q: What is Employment News?**
A: Employment News is a weekly newspaper published by the Government of India in both English and Hindi ('Rozgar Samachar'). It provides the latest government job vacancies from Central, State Governments, Public Sector Undertakings, Autonomous Bodies, Universities, alongside admission notices for professional courses, examination notices, and exam results.

**Q: How to get Employment News Paper Online?**
A: Websites like IndGovtJobs.in update all the latest Employment News paper job details and top vacancies. For official access, you can visit the Employment News e-version official website `e-employmentnews.co.in`.

**Q: How to Subscribe to Employment News?**
A: Candidates can visit the official Employment News website (`www.employmentnews.gov.in`), click on the 'e-version' tab or `en.eversion` to subscribe online for weekly newspapers. Various packages like 6-Months, 1-year, 2-years, and 3-years are available.

**Q: What is the Employment News paper subscription cost?**
A: The subscription costs are generally ₹400 for 01 year, ₹750 for 02 years, and ₹1050 for 03 years.

**Q: What types of jobs are available in Employment News?**
A: Employment News publishes job opportunities and recruitment examinations for candidates with minimum qualifications ranging from 8th, 10th, 12th Pass to Degree and Post Graduates. Posts include Assistants, Clerks, Officers, Engineers, Executives, Managers, Group A, Group B, Group C, Gazetted and Non-Gazetted positions across various Ministries in Central/State Government, Public Sector Undertakings, Railway, and Banking sectors.

**Q: What are the leading job advertisements in Employment News Paper?**
A: The leading job advertisements typically include those from the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC), Staff Selection Commission (SSC), various Central Government Ministries and Departments, Railway Recruitment Boards (RRB), Public Sector Banks (IBPS), Defence organizations, and State Government vacancies.

content:
## HRRL Recruitment 2026: Your Gateway to a Promising Career

HPCL Rajasthan Refinery Limited (HRRL) invites online applications for 134 Fresher & Experienced posts. This is an exceptional opportunity to join a prestigious joint venture project between Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) and the Government of Rajasthan. The recruitment drive covers a wide range of regular and fixed-term positions, offering diverse roles in critical engineering, management, and support functions.

### Vacancy Details (Total: 134 Posts)

The vacancies are broadly categorized into Regular and Fixed Term posts, spanning various disciplines and grades.

#### HRRL Regular Vacancy 2026 Post Wise List (Total: 129 Posts)

| Post Name | Grade | No. of Vacancies |
| :---------------------------------------------- | :---- | :--------------- |
| Jr. Executive – Chemical | E0 | 37 |
| Asst. Engineer – Chemical | E1 | 10 |
| Engineer – Chemical | E2 | 31 |
| Sr. Engineer – Process (Refinery/Petrochemical) | E3 | 10 |
| Sr. Manager – Technical Planning | E5 | 01 |
| Sr. Manager – Process | E5 | 02 |
| Jr. Executive – Mechanical | E0 | 01 |
| Engineer – Mechanical | E2 | 01 |
| Sr. Engineer – Mechanical | E3 | 04 |
| Sr. Manager – Mechanical | E5 | 05 |
| Engineer – Electrical | E2 | 03 |
| Sr. Manager – Electrical | E5 | 02 |
| Asst. Engineer – Instrumentation | E1 | 01 |
| Engineer – Instrumentation | E2 | 02 |
| Sr. Manager – Instrumentation | E5 | 04 |
| Asst. Accounts Officer | E1 | 04 |
| Accounts Officer | E2 | 02 |
| Sr. Manager – Finance | E5 | 05 |
| Medical Officer | E2 | 01 |
| Asst. Officer – Information Systems | E1 | 01 |
| Jr. Executive – Fire & Safety | E0 | 02 |

#### HRRL Fixed Term Vacancy 2026 Post Wise List (Total: 5 Posts)

| Post Name | No. of Vacancies |
| :------------------------------------------- | :--------------- |
| Plant Head – HDPE/LLDPE Swing | 01 |
| Plant Head – Polypropylene (PPU) | 01 |
| Extruder Head – HDPE/LLDPE & PP | 01 |
| Plant Head – Dual Feed Cracker Unit (DFCU) & Allied Units | 01 |
| Plant Head – Delayed Coker Unit (DCU) | 01 |

### Post-wise Eligibility Criteria & Age Limits

The eligibility criteria, including educational qualifications, required experience, and maximum age limit, vary for each post and grade. The cut-off date for age and experience calculation is **May 13, 2026**.

| Post Name | Grade | Educational Qualification | Experience | Max Age (as on 13.05.2026) |
| :---------------------------------------------- | :---- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :---------------------------------- | :-------------------------- |
| Jr. Executive – Chemical | E0 | 3-year Diploma in Chemical / Petrochemical Engg. or B.Sc. Chemistry (60%/50%) | Nil | 25 yrs |
| Asst. Engineer – Chemical | E1 | B.E./B.Tech Chemical / Petrochemical (60%/50%) | Nil | 25 yrs |
| Engineer – Chemical | E2 | B.E./B.Tech Chemical / Petrochemical (60%/50%) | 3 years (Refinery/Petrochemical) | 29 yrs |
| Sr. Engineer – Process | E3 | B.E./B.Tech Chemical / Petrochemical (60%/50%) | 6 years (Refinery/Petrochemical) | 34 yrs |
| Sr. Manager – Technical Planning / Process | E5 | B.E./B.Tech Chemical / Petrochemical / Petroleum (60%/50%) | 12 years | 42 yrs |
| Jr. Executive – Mechanical | E0 | 3-year Diploma Mechanical (60%/50%) | Nil | 25 yrs |
| Engineer / Sr. Engineer / Sr. Manager – Mechanical | E2/E3/E5 | B.E./B.Tech Mechanical (60%/50%) | 3/6/12 years | 29/34/42 yrs |
| Engineer / Sr. Manager – Electrical | E2/E5 | B.E./B.Tech Electrical (60%/50%) | 3/12 years | 29/42 yrs |
| Asst. Engineer / Engineer / Sr. Manager – Instrumentation | E1/E2/E5 | B.E./B.Tech Instrumentation / Electronics (60%/50%) | Nil/3/12 years | 25/29/42 yrs |
| Asst. Accounts Officer / Accounts Officer / Sr. Manager – Finance | E1/E2/E5 | Qualified CA (ICAI) | Nil/3/12 years | 25/29/42 yrs |
| Medical Officer | E2 | MBBS + AFIH | 2 years | 29 yrs |
| Asst. Officer – Information Systems | E1 | B.E./B.Tech CS/IT or MCA/MBA (IT) | Nil | 25 yrs |
| Jr. Executive – Fire & Safety | E0 | Diploma / Science Graduate + Heavy Vehicle Driving License | Nil | 25 yrs |
| Fixed Term Posts (Plant Heads) | - | B.E./B.Tech Chemical / Mechanical / Polymer | 17-20 years experience (details in official notification) | - |

*Note: Percentage requirements are typically 60% for UR/OBC/EWS candidates and 50% for SC/ST/PwBD candidates. Age relaxations for reserved categories will be applicable as per Government of India norms.* 

### Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application fee as per their category:

| Category | Application Fee |
| :------------------ | :----------------------------- |
| UR / OBC-NCL / EWS | ₹1180/- (inclusive of ₹1000 + 18% GST + gateway charges) |
| SC / ST / PwBD | Nil |

**Payment Mode:** The application fee must be paid online through Debit/Credit Card, UPI, or Net Banking.

### Selection Process

The selection process for HRRL Recruitment 2026 is designed to identify the most suitable candidates for each position and varies by grade:

*   **E0 Posts:** Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by a Skill Test and Personal Interview.
*   **E1 & E2 Posts:** Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by a Group Task / Group Discussion and Personal Interview.
*   **E3 & E5 Posts:** Scrutiny of Application & Resume followed by Technical & Behavioral Competency Interview.

### Important Dates

Candidates should mark these crucial dates for the HRRL Recruitment 2026 application process:

| Event | Date |
| :------------------------------- | :----------------------------- |
| Commencement of Online Application | 14 May 2026 (10:00 hrs) |
| Last Date for Online Application & Fee Payment | 03 June 2026 (23:45 hrs) |
| Eligibility / Age / Experience Cut-off Date | 13 May 2026 |

### How to Apply for HRRL Recruitment 2026?

Eligible and interested candidates must apply online through the dedicated HRRL recruitment portal. Follow these steps carefully to ensure a successful application:

1.  **Step 1: Visit the Official Portal:** Navigate to the official HRRL careers portal.
2.  **Step 2: Register:** Register yourself by providing a valid email address and mobile number. You will receive login credentials.
3.  **Step 3: Fill Application Form:** Log in and meticulously fill out the online application form with all required personal, educational, experience, and category details.
4.  **Step 4: Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your recent photograph, signature, academic certificates, experience letters, and other necessary documents as per the specifications mentioned in the official notification.
5.  **Step 5: Pay Fee & Submit:** If applicable, proceed to pay the application fee online. After successful payment (or if exempt), review all details and submit the application form.
6.  **Step 6: Print Confirmation:** Take a printout of the finally submitted application form for your records and future reference.

### Important Links

*   **Official HRRL Website:** [https://hrrl.in/](https://hrrl.in/)
*   **Detailed Notification PDF:** _(Available on the official HRRL website)_ 
*   **Apply Online Link:** _(Available on the official HRRL website during application period)_

meta_description: The CSIR-Central Institute of Mining and Fuel Research (CIMFR), operating under the Ministry of Science & Technology, Government of India, has announced a
content:
# CIMFR Technician Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 30 Posts

The CSIR-Central Institute of Mining and Fuel Research (CIMFR), a premier laboratory under the Ministry of Science & Technology, Government of India, has released Advertisement No. CIMFR-02/2026 for the recruitment of 30 Technician-1 (Group-II) posts in various trades. This is an exceptional opportunity for qualified individuals seeking a career in a reputed central government organization.

## Important Dates

| Event | Date & Time |
| :----------------------------------- | :-------------------------- |
| Commencement of Online Application | 20 May 2026 (10:00 AM) |
| Last Date for Submission of Online Application | 19 June 2026 (17:00 hours) |
| Last Date for Payment of Application Fee | 19 June 2026 (17:00 hours) |

## Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay the application fee as per their category. The fee is payable online through SBI Collect.

| Category | Fee | Remarks |
| :---------------------- | :-------- | :---------------------------------- |
| General / OBC / EWS | ₹500/- | Bank charges extra |
| SC / ST / PwBD / Women / ESM | Exempted (Nil) | No fee required |

## Vacancy Details

A total of 30 Technician-1 (Group-II) posts are available across various trades, with reservations as detailed below:

| Post Code | Discipline / Trade | Total Posts | Reservation Details |
| :-------- | :----------------------- | :---------- | :---------------------------------- |
| 2026/001 | Instrument Mechanics | 03 | UR:01, EWS:01, ST:01 |
| 2026/002 | Survey / Surveyor / Surveying | 03 | UR:01, EWS:01, ST:01 |
| 2026/003 | Fitter | 05 | UR:02, SC:01, ST:02 (1 PwBD-VH) |
| 2026/004 | Welder | 02 | UR:01, ST:01 |
| 2026/005 | Carpenter | 01 | OBC:01 |
| 2026/006 | Electrician | 07 | UR:03, EWS:01, ST:03 (1 ESM + 1 PwBD-HH) |
| 2026/007 | Wireman | 01 | OBC:01 |
| 2026/008 | Refrigeration & Air Conditioning (RAC) | 02 | UR:01, ST:01 |
| 2026/009 | Draughtsman (Civil) | 01 | EWS:01 (ESM) |
| 2026/010 | Automobile / Mechanic Motor Vehicle (MMV) | 02 | UR:01, ST:01 (ESM) |
| 2026/011 | Draughtsman (Mechanical) | 03 | EWS:01, SC:01, ST:01 (ESM) |
| **Grand Total** | | **30** | **+ Horizontal PwBD-02 & ESM-03** |

## Eligibility Criteria

### Educational Qualification (Common for all trades)

Applicants must possess the following qualifications as of the last date of application (June 19, 2026):

*   **SSC/10th with Science subjects** with a minimum of 55% marks.
*   **AND** one of the following:
    *   ITI/National/State Trade Certificate in the relevant trade.
    *   2 years full-time Apprenticeship in the relevant trade.
    *   3 years experience in the relevant trade in a Government/PSU/Autonomous Body.

### Age Limit (as on 19.06.2026)

*   **Minimum Age:** 18 years
*   **Maximum Age:** 28 years

**Age Relaxation:**
*   SC/ST candidates: 5 years
*   OBC(NCL) candidates: 3 years
*   PwBD candidates: 10/13/15 years (depending on category)
*   ESM candidates: As per Government rules
*   Departmental candidates: 5 years
*   Widows/Divorced Women: Up to 35 years (General), 38 years (OBC), 40 years (SC/ST)

## Pay Scale & Emoluments

Selected candidates will be placed at Pay Level-2 as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC).

*   **Pay Level-2:** ₹19,900 – ₹63,200
*   **Total Emoluments:** Approximately ₹37,000/- per month (including HRA and other admissible allowances in Dhanbad).
*   **Additional Benefits:** Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance (TA), Medical facilities, National Pension System (NPS), and other CSIR benefits as per rules.

## Selection Process

The selection process for CIMFR Technician-1 (Group-II) posts will comprise the following stages:

1.  **Screening of Applications:** Initial scrutiny of submitted applications.
2.  **Trade Test:** A qualifying test to assess practical skills in the relevant trade.
3.  **Written Examination:** Comprising three papers (Paper-I, Paper-II, Paper-III).
4.  **Document Verification:** Verification of all original documents for eligible candidates.

## Exam Pattern

The written examination will be conducted in OMR or Computer Based Test (CBT) mode.

| Paper | Duration | Subject | No. of Questions | Marks | Negative Marking |
| :---- | :-------- | :-------------------- | :--------------- | :---- | :--------------- |
| I | 1 Hour | Mental Ability Test | 50 | 100 | No negative |
| II | 30 Min | General Awareness + English Language | 25+25 | 150 | 1 mark per wrong |
| III | 1 Hour | Concerned Subject / Trade | 50 | 150 | 1 mark per wrong |
| **Total** | **2½ Hours** | | **150** | **400** | | 

*   **Total Questions:** 150
*   **Total Duration:** 2 Hours 30 Minutes
*   **Standard:** SSC + ITI / 12th Level

## How to Apply

Eligible and interested candidates must apply online through the official CIMFR website by the stipulated deadline. Follow these steps:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to www.cimfr.res.in.
2.  **Registration:** Register on the portal using a valid Email ID and create a secure password.
3.  **Fill Application Form:** Complete the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and experience details. Ensure to select the correct Post Code for the desired trade.
4.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph, signature, and all other required certificates/documents as per the specified format and size.
5.  **Pay Application Fee:** If applicable to your category, proceed to pay the application fee of ₹500/- through SBI Collect. Upload the generated payment receipt.
6.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all entered information before final submission.
7.  **Print Confirmation:** After successful submission, take a printout of the filled application form and confirmation page for your records.

## Important Links

*   **CIMFR Official Website:** [www.cimfr.res.in](https://www.cimfr.res.in)

content:
### Indian Navy B.Tech Entry Scheme (10+2) January 2027 Batch Recruitment

The Indian Navy invites applications from eligible unmarried male candidates for the prestigious B.Tech Entry Scheme for its January 2027 batch. This scheme offers a unique opportunity for young aspirants to pursue a B.Tech degree from the Indian Naval Academy (INA), Ezhimala, and simultaneously embark on a challenging and rewarding career as a commissioned officer in the Indian Navy. Successful candidates will undergo a comprehensive 4-year B.Tech course under the 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry Scheme.

#### Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the Indian Navy B.Tech Entry Scheme (10+2), candidates must meet the following criteria:

*   **Educational Qualification:**
    *   Passed 10+2 (Intermediate) examination with at least 70% aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics (PCM).
    *   Obtained at least 50% marks in English (either in Class X or Class XII).
    *   Must have appeared for the Joint Entrance Examination (JEE) Main examination.
*   **Age Limit:** Candidates must be born between 02 July 2004 and 01 July 2007 (both dates inclusive), which typically translates to an age of 16½ to 19½ years at the time of entry. *Exact age criteria will be specified in the official notification.*
*   **Marital Status:** Unmarried male candidates only.

#### Vacancy Details

The exact number of vacancies for the January 2027 batch will be released with the detailed official notification. These positions are typically allocated for Executive, Engineering, and Electrical branches of the Indian Navy.

#### Application Fee

The application fee details will be announced in the official notification. Based on previous recruitments, general categories might have a fee, while some categories could be exempt.

| Category         | Application Fee |
| :--------------- | :-------------- |
| General/OBC      | To be notified  |
| SC/ST/PwBD/Women | To be notified  |
| Payment Mode     | Online          |

#### Important Dates & Schedule

Candidates should regularly check the official Indian Navy website for the most up-to-date information regarding application dates and the selection process.

| Event                         | Date (Expected)         |
| :---------------------------- | :---------------------- |
| Notification Release Date     | To be announced (2026)  |
| Online Application Start Date | To be announced (2026)  |
| Online Application End Date   | To be announced (2026)  |
| JEE (Main) Exam               | As per NTA schedule     |
| SSB Interview Dates           | To be announced         |
| Merit List Release            | To be announced         |

#### Selection Process

The selection process for the Indian Navy B.Tech Entry Scheme typically involves the following stages:

1.  **Shortlisting of Applications:** Candidates will be shortlisted based on their JEE (Main) rank. A cut-off rank will be decided by the Integrated Headquarters of MoD (Navy).
2.  **SSB Interview:** Shortlisted candidates will be called for a Service Selection Board (SSB) interview at various centers across India (Bangalore, Bhopal, Kolkata, Visakhapatnam). The SSB interview lasts for five days and comprises two stages:
    *   **Stage I:** Officer Intelligence Rating (OIR) Test and Picture Perception & Discussion Test (PP&DT).
    *   **Stage II:** Psychological Tests, Group Testing Officer (GTO) Tasks, Personal Interview, and Conference.
3.  **Medical Examination:** Candidates recommended by the SSB will undergo a detailed medical examination as per naval standards.
4.  **Merit List:** A final merit list will be prepared based on the SSB interview marks.

#### How to Apply

Interested and eligible candidates must apply online through the official website of the Indian Navy. Follow the steps below to complete your application:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official Indian Navy recruitment website (joinindiannavy.gov.in).
2.  **Register/Login:** If you are a new user, click on 'Register' and create an account using a valid email ID and mobile number. If already registered, log in to your account.
3.  **Fill Application Form:** Navigate to the 'Current Opportunities' section and find the '10+2 B.Tech Entry Scheme' notification. Click on 'Apply Now'.
4.  **Enter Details:** Fill in all the required personal, educational, and JEE (Main) details accurately.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, 10th mark sheet, 12th mark sheet, and JEE (Main) score card as per the specified format and size.
6.  **Pay Application Fee:** If applicable, proceed to pay the application fee online using Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking.
7.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the entered information before final submission. No changes will be allowed after submission.
8.  **Print Application:** After successful submission, download and take a printout of the application form for future reference.

#### Important Links

| Link Type             | URL                                       |
| :-------------------- | :---------------------------------------- |
| Official Website      | [joinindiannavy.gov.in](https://joinindiannavy.gov.in/) |
| Official Notification | To be released soon                       |
| Apply Online          | To be activated soon                      |

overview: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) is set to release the Combined Graduate Level (CGL) Examination 2026 notification, a highly anticipated opportunity for lakhs of aspirants seeking esteemed Group 'B' and Group 'C' posts in various Ministries, Departments, and Organizations of the Government of India. With an expected 12,256 vacancies, this examination is a gateway to a rewarding career for graduates across the nation. eligibility: Candidates must possess a Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University or equivalent. Specific educational qualifications may vary for certain posts such as Junior Statistical Officer (JSO), Statistical Investigator Grade-II, and Assistant Audit Officer/Assistant Accounts Officer.
age_limit: Candidates' age should generally be between 18 to 32 years, depending on the post. Age relaxation will be applicable as per Government of India rules for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/ESM).
salary: The salary varies significantly based on the post, ranging from Pay Level-4 (₹25,500 - ₹81,100) to Pay Level-8 (₹47,600 - ₹1,51,100) as per the 7th Pay Commission.
how_to_apply: The application process for SSC CGL 2026 will be entirely online. meta_description: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) is set to release the Combined Graduate Level (CGL) Examination 2026 notification, a highly anticipated opportunity fo
content:
## SSC CGL 2026 Notification: Your Gateway to Central Government Careers

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) is gearing up to release the Combined Graduate Level (CGL) Examination 2026 notification, offering a significant opportunity for graduates to secure prestigious Group 'B' and Group 'C' positions within various Ministries, Departments, and Organizations of the Government of India. With an anticipated 12,256 vacancies, this is a prime chance to build a stable and respected career.

### Important Dates & Examination Schedule

While the official dates for SSC CGL 2026 are yet to be announced, candidates can refer to the typical timeline for this examination. Stay tuned to the official SSC website for precise dates.

| Event | Tentative Dates | Details |
| :------------------------- | :----------------------------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Notification Release | Expected Late 2025 | Official notification with detailed eligibility, vacancies, and application process. |
| Application Start Date | Expected Late 2025 / Early 2026 | Candidates can begin submitting their online applications. |
| Application End Date | Expected Early 2026 | Last date for online application submission. |
| Tier-I Exam Date | Expected Mid-2026 | Computer Based Examination (CBE) - Objective Type. |
| Tier-II Exam Date | Expected Late 2026 | Computer Based Examination (CBE) - Objective Type for various papers. |
| Result Declaration | To be announced | Results for each tier will be released progressively. |

### Application Fee Details

Applicants are required to pay a non-refundable application fee. Fee exemption is available for certain categories as per government norms.

| Category | Fee (₹) | Payment Mode |
| :------------------- | :---------- | :------------------------------------------------------ |
| General / OBC (Male) | 100         | Online (BHIM UPI, Net Banking, Visa, Mastercard, Maestro Credit/Debit cards) |
| SC / ST / PwBD / ESM | Exempted    | No Fee |
| Women Candidates     | Exempted    | No Fee |

### Vacancy Details

The SSC CGL 2026 notification is expected to announce approximately 12,256 vacancies across various posts. These posts are distributed among different Ministries/Departments/Organizations of the Government of India. A detailed breakdown of vacancies per post and category will be provided in the official notification.

### Post-wise Qualifications and Age Limits

The eligibility criteria for SSC CGL are primarily based on a Bachelor's Degree. However, certain posts have specific educational or age requirements. All age limits are calculated as on the crucial cut-off date mentioned in the official notification.

#### General Eligibility for Most Posts:
*   **Educational Qualification:** Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University or equivalent.
*   **Age Limit:** Generally between 18-27 years, 18-30 years, or 18-32 years depending on the specific post. 

#### Specific Post Requirements:
*   **Assistant Audit Officer/Assistant Accounts Officer (AAO):** Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University or Institute. **Desirable Qualification:** Chartered Accountant or Cost & Management Accountant or Company Secretary or Masters in Commerce or Masters in Business Studies or Masters in Business Administration (Finance) or Masters in Business Economics.
    *   **Age Limit:** Not exceeding 30 years.
*   **Junior Statistical Officer (JSO):** Bachelor's Degree in any subject from a recognized University or Institute with at least 60% Marks in Mathematics at 12th Standard OR Bachelor's Degree in any subject with Statistics as one of the subjects at degree level.
    *   **Age Limit:** 18-32 years.
*   **Statistical Investigator Grade-II:** Bachelor's Degree in any subject with Statistics as one of the subjects from a recognized University or equivalent.
    *   **Age Limit:** Not exceeding 30 years.
*   **All other Posts:** Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University or equivalent.
    *   **Age Limit:** Varies from 18-27 years, 18-30 years, or 20-30 years, depending on the post.

**Age Relaxation:**
*   SC/ST: 5 years
*   OBC: 3 years
*   PwBD (Unreserved): 10 years
*   PwBD (OBC): 13 years
*   PwBD (SC/ST): 15 years
*   Ex-Servicemen: 3 years after deduction of the military service rendered from the actual age as on the closing date.

### Step-by-Step How to Apply

Follow these instructions to successfully apply for the SSC CGL 2026 examination:

1.  **One-Time Registration:** If you are a new user, visit the official SSC website (ssc.nic.in) and complete the 'One-Time Registration' process. You will need to provide basic details, contact information, and upload a scanned photograph and signature as per specifications.
2.  **Login:** After successful registration, log in to the SSC portal using your Registration Number and Password.
3.  **Find Application Link:** Navigate to the 'Apply' section and find the link for 'Combined Graduate Level Examination 2026'.
4.  **Fill Application Form:** Carefully fill in all the required details in the online application form. Review all information before proceeding.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload your latest passport-size photograph (taken within three months, without cap or spectacles, and ensuring the date on which the photograph has been taken is clearly printed on the photograph) and signature as per the specifications mentioned in the official notification.
6.  **Select Exam Centers:** Choose three preferred examination centers in order of priority.
7.  **Pay Application Fee:** If applicable to your category, proceed to pay the application fee online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or by using Visa, Mastercard, Maestro Credit/Debit cards. Candidates eligible for fee exemption do not need to pay.
8.  **Review and Submit:** Thoroughly review your entire application form for any errors or discrepancies. Once satisfied, click on the 'Submit' button.
9.  **Print Application:** After submission, take a printout of the filled application form for your records. It is not required to send a hard copy to SSC.

Candidates are strongly advised to read the official notification carefully once it is released to understand all specific requirements and instructions.

content:
# CTET September 2026: Online Application Details

The Central Teacher Eligibility Test (CTET) is a national-level examination conducted by the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) to determine the eligibility of candidates for appointment as teachers for Classes 1st to 8th in central government schools and other schools across India.

## Important Dates

Candidates are advised to keep track of the following crucial dates for the CTET September 2026 examination:

| Event                      | Date (Tentative)          |
| :------------------------- | :------------------------ |
| Online Application Start   | July 01, 2026             |
| Online Application End     | July 31, 2026             |
| Last Date for Fee Payment  | July 31, 2026             |
| Correction Window Opens    | August 05, 2026           |
| Correction Window Closes   | August 10, 2026           |
| Admit Card Release         | Last week of August 2026  |
| Examination Date           | September 2026            |
| Result Declaration         | October 2026              |

*Note: The examination date in September 2026 will be announced in the official notification.*

## Application Fee

The application fee for CTET September 2026 is as follows. Candidates must pay the fee online through the official portal.

| Category                 | For Paper I or Paper II | For Both Paper I & II |
| :----------------------- | :---------------------- | :-------------------- |
| General / OBC            | ₹1000/-                 | ₹1200/-               |
| SC / ST / Differently Abled | ₹500/-                  | ₹600/-                |

*Note: Transaction charges may apply extra.*

## Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible for CTET September 2026, candidates must meet the educational qualifications prescribed by the National Council for Teacher Education (NCTE). The primary criteria are:

**For Primary Stage (Paper I - Classes I-V):**
*   Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education.
*   Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education, in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure), Regulations 2002.
*   Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 4-year Bachelor of Elementary Education (B.El.Ed).
*   Graduation with at least 50% marks and Bachelor of Education (B.Ed).

**For Elementary Stage (Paper II - Classes VI-VIII):**
*   Graduation and passed or appearing in final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education.
*   Graduation with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in Bachelor in Education (B.Ed).
*   Graduation with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in Bachelor in Education (B.Ed), in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure) Regulations issued from time to time in this regard.
*   Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 4-year Bachelor in Elementary Education (B.El.Ed).
*   Graduation with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in 1-year B.Ed. (Special Education).

*Refer to the official CTET information bulletin for detailed and updated eligibility norms.*

## Age Limit

There is **no upper age limit** for applying for the CTET examination. Candidates who meet the educational qualification criteria are eligible to apply irrespective of their age.

## How to Apply

Interested and eligible candidates can apply online for CTET September 2026 by following the steps outlined below:

1.  **Access the Official Website:** Navigate to the official CTET website, `ctet.nic.in`.
2.  **Locate 'Apply Online' Link:** Find and click on the 'Apply for CTET September 2026' link available on the homepage.
3.  **New Registration:** If you are a first-time applicant, register by providing your name, father's name, mother's name, date of birth, email ID, mobile number, and create a strong password. Note down the system-generated Application Number.
4.  **Fill Application Form:** Log in using your Application Number and Password. Proceed to fill out the detailed application form. This includes personal details, contact information, educational qualifications, and choice of examination centers.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned images of your passport-sized photograph and signature as per the specifications mentioned in the information bulletin (size, dimensions, format).
6.  **Select Paper(s) and Subject:** Choose whether you wish to apply for Paper I, Paper II, or both. For Paper II, select your optional subjects (Mathematics & Science or Social Science).
7.  **Pay Examination Fee:** Complete the fee payment through the available online modes (Credit Card/Debit Card/Net Banking). Ensure you receive a confirmation of successful payment.
8.  **Review and Final Submission:** Carefully review all the details entered in the application form before final submission. Once submitted, changes may not be possible.
9.  **Print Confirmation Page:** After successful submission and payment, download and print the confirmation page. Keep it safe for future reference.

## Important Links

| Link Type        | URL                           |
| :--------------- | :---------------------------- |
| Apply Online     | https://ctet.nic.in/ApplyOnline/   |
| Official Website | https://ctet.nic.in/          |

eligibility: Candidates must have passed 10+2 (Intermediate) examination with at least 70% aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM) and a minimum of 50% marks in English (either in Class 10th or Class 12th). Additionally, candidates must have appeared for the JEE (Main) examination in the specified year (e.g., 2026 for this batch) to be eligible for shortlisting.

salary: Upon successful completion of training, candidates will be commissioned as Sub Lieutenant in Level 10 of the Defence Pay Matrix (₹56,100 - ₹1,77,500) as per 7th CPC, along with Military Service Pay (MSP) and other allowances.

meta_description: The Indian Navy has announced the B.Tech Entry Scheme (10+2) for the January 2027 batch, inviting bright and motivated candidates to join as Permanent Comm
content:
### Indian Navy B.Tech Entry (10+2) Recruitment 2026 for Jan 2027 Batch

The Indian Navy offers a golden opportunity for young and dynamic individuals to join its ranks as Permanent Commission officers through the B.Tech Entry Scheme (10+2). This program allows selected candidates to pursue a Bachelor of Technology degree at the prestigious Indian Naval Academy, Ezhimala, while simultaneously undergoing comprehensive naval training. Aspiring officers will embark on a challenging yet rewarding career, contributing to national security and leading a life of adventure and distinction.

#### Vacancy Details

*   **Total Posts:** To be notified in the official notification. (Typically around 30-40 vacancies for different branches like Executive, Engineering, Electrical, etc.)

#### Important Dates & Schedule

| Event                          | Tentative Date / Period            |
| :----------------------------- | :--------------------------------- |
| Online Application Start Date  | To be notified (Usually Oct-Nov 2025) |
| Online Application End Date    | To be notified (Usually Nov-Dec 2025) |
| SSB Interview Dates            | Feb-Apr 2026                       |
| Merit List Declaration         | May-Jun 2026                       |
| Commencement of Training       | January 2027                       |

#### Application Fee

| Category                | Fee Amount (Tentative) |
| :---------------------- | :--------------------- |
| General/OBC             | ₹0 (No Application Fee) |
| SC/ST                   | ₹0 (No Application Fee) |
| **Payment Mode:**       | Not Applicable         |

*Note: Application for the Indian Navy B.Tech Entry Scheme is typically free for all candidates.*

#### Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet the following criteria to be eligible for the B.Tech Entry (10+2) Scheme:

*   **Educational Qualification:**
    *   Passed 10+2 (Intermediate) or equivalent examination from any Board recognized by Central/State Government.
    *   Secured at least 70% aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM).
    *   Obtained a minimum of 50% marks in English (either in Class 10th or Class 12th).
    *   **JEE (Main) Requirement:** Candidates must have appeared for the JEE (Main) examination in the specified year (e.g., JEE (Main) 2026 for this batch). Shortlisting for SSB interviews will be based on JEE (Main) All India Rank (AIR).
*   **Age Limit:**
    *   Candidates must be born between **02 July 2007 and 01 January 2010** (both dates inclusive) for the January 2027 batch.
    *   No age relaxation is generally provided for this entry scheme.
*   **Gender:** Male and Female candidates are eligible to apply.
*   **Marital Status:** Unmarried candidates are eligible.

#### Selection Process

The selection process for the Indian Navy B.Tech Entry (10+2) scheme typically involves the following stages:

1.  **Shortlisting based on JEE (Main) Rank:** Candidates will be shortlisted for SSB interviews based on their JEE (Main) All India Rank.
2.  **SSB Interview:** Shortlisted candidates will undergo a five-day Service Selection Board (SSB) interview process, which includes:
    *   **Stage I:** Intelligence Test, Picture Perception and Discussion Test (PP&DT).
    *   **Stage II:** Psychological Tests, Group Testing Officer (GTO) Tasks, Personal Interview, and Conference.
3.  **Medical Examination:** Candidates recommended by the SSB will undergo a detailed medical examination as per naval standards.
4.  **Merit List:** A final merit list will be prepared based on the SSB interview performance and medical fitness.

#### How to Apply

Interested and eligible candidates must apply online through the official website of the Indian Navy. Follow these steps carefully:

1.  **Visit the Official Indian Navy Recruitment Website:** Go to [www.joinindiannavy.gov.in](https://www.joinindiannavy.gov.in).
2.  **Register/Login:**
    *   If you are a new user, click on 'Register' and create an account by providing a valid email ID and mobile number.
    *   If already registered, log in using your registered email ID/mobile number and password.
3.  **Fill Application Form:**
    *   Click on the 'Apply Online' link for the 'B.Tech Entry Scheme (10+2) - Jan 2027 Batch'.
    *   Fill in all mandatory details accurately, including personal information, educational qualifications (10th, 12th, and JEE Main details), and contact information.
4.  **Upload Documents:**
    *   Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-sized photograph, signature, 10th class certificate, 12th class mark sheet, and JEE (Main) scorecard (if available).
    *   Ensure all documents are in the prescribed format and size.
5.  **Review and Submit:**
    *   Carefully review all the information entered in the application form before final submission. Make sure there are no errors.
    *   Click on the 'Submit' button.
6.  **Print Application:**
    *   After successful submission, download and take a printout of the application form for your records and future reference.
    *   No need to send a hard copy of the application form to the Indian Navy unless specifically instructed.

Candidates are advised to regularly check the official Indian Navy website for the latest updates and detailed notification.

content:
## DRDO Paid Internship 2026

The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) invites applications for its Paid Internship 2026 at various laboratories, including DLJ (Jodhpur) and ITR (Chandipur). This internship provides a unique opportunity to work on defence-related projects under expert guidance.

### Application Fee
| Category | Fee |
|----------|-----|
| General/OBC | ₹0 |
| SC/ST/PwD | ₹0 |
| All Female | ₹0 |

*No application fee is required for DRDO internships.*

### Important Dates
| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Start of Online Application | 1 January 2026 |
| Last Date to Apply | 31 March 2026 |
| Internship Duration | 6 months (tentative) |

### Eligibility Criteria
- **Educational Qualification:** Candidates must be pursuing or have completed B.E./B.Tech, M.E./M.Tech, or M.Sc. in relevant fields (e.g., Electronics, Mechanical, Computer Science, Physics, Chemistry) from a recognized university/institute.
- **Minimum Marks:** Typically 60% aggregate or equivalent CGPA.
- **Age Limit:** No specific age limit; however, candidates should be within the age range suitable for internship (usually up to 28 years).

### Stipend
Selected interns will receive a monthly stipend as per DRDO norms:
- B.E./B.Tech: ₹10,000 - ₹15,000
- M.E./M.Tech: ₹20,000 - ₹30,000
- M.Sc.: ₹15,000 - ₹20,000

### How to Apply
1. Visit the official DRDO website or the specific lab's career page.
2. Click on the 'Internship 2026' notification.
3. Register with a valid email ID and phone number.
4. Fill in the application form with accurate details.
5. Upload scanned copies of mark sheets, resume, and a project proposal (if required).
6. Submit the application and take a printout for future reference.

### Selection Process
- Shortlisting based on academic records and project proposal.
- Interview (may be conducted online or in-person).
- Final selection based on performance.

### Important Links
- **Official Website:** [DRDO](https://www.drdo.gov.in)
- **Notification PDF:** Check the official site for updates.

*For more details, refer to the official notification.*

content:
### BPSC 72nd Combined Competitive Examination 2026: Upcoming Notification

The Bihar Public Service Commission (BPSC) is expected to release the detailed notification for its 72nd Combined Competitive Examination (CCE) for the year 2026. This examination is a gateway for eligible candidates to secure various administrative and civil service positions across different departments under the Bihar State Government. Aspirants are advised to keep an eye on the official BPSC website for the most up-to-date information regarding this prestigious recruitment drive.

#### Important Dates & Schedule

| Event                          | Date (Tentative/To Be Announced) |
| :----------------------------- | :------------------------------- |
| Official Notification Release  | To Be Announced                  |
| Online Application Start Date  | To Be Announced                  |
| Online Application End Date    | To Be Announced                  |
| Last Date for Fee Payment      | To Be Announced                  |
| Prelims Exam Date              | To Be Announced                  |
| Mains Exam Date                | To Be Announced                  |
| Interview Date                 | To Be Announced                  |

#### Vacancy Details

The official notification will provide a comprehensive list of department-wise and category-wise vacancies for the 72nd BPSC Combined Competitive Examination 2026. Posts typically include:

*   Deputy Collector
*   Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP)
*   Commercial Tax Officer
*   Bihar Education Service
*   Rural Development Officer
*   District Minority Welfare Officer
*   Supply Inspector
*   And various other Group A and Group B posts.

Detailed vacancy distribution will be available upon the release of the official advertisement.

#### Eligibility Criteria

**1. Educational Qualification:**
Candidates must possess a Bachelor's Degree or an equivalent qualification from a University recognized by the Government of India. Specific post-wise educational requirements, if any, will be detailed in the official notification.

**2. Age Limit (as on a specified cut-off date, to be announced):**
*   **Minimum Age:** 20/21 years (depending on the post).
*   **Maximum Age (General Male):** 37 years.
*   **Maximum Age (General Female, OBC Male/Female):** 40 years.
*   **Maximum Age (SC/ST Male/Female):** 42 years.
*   Age relaxation will be applicable as per Bihar Government rules for reserved categories.

#### Application Fee

The application fee for the BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 is expected to be as follows, subject to change in the official notification:

| Category                              | Application Fee (Expected) |
| :------------------------------------ | :------------------------- |
| General/OBC/Other State Candidates    | ₹600/-                     |
| SC/ST/PwD/Female Candidates (Bihar Domicile) | ₹150/-                     |
| **Payment Mode:**                     | Online (Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking) |

*Additional bank charges may apply.*

#### Selection Process

The selection process for the BPSC Combined Competitive Examination generally comprises three stages:

1.  **Preliminary Examination (Prelims):** A qualifying objective-type examination consisting of one General Studies paper.
2.  **Main Examination (Mains):** A descriptive-type examination comprising General Hindi (qualifying), General Studies Papers I, II, and an Optional Subject.
3.  **Interview:** A personality test conducted for candidates who qualify the Main Examination.

The final merit list is prepared based on the marks obtained in the Main Examination and Interview.

#### How to Apply

Interested and eligible candidates will be able to apply online through the official website of the Bihar Public Service Commission. Follow these general steps:

1.  **Visit the Official BPSC Website:** Go to `bpsc.bih.nic.in` once the application window opens.
2.  **Locate the Notification:** Find and click on the link for "72nd Combined Competitive Examination 2026".
3.  **Online Registration:** If you are a new user, complete the registration process by providing basic details. You will receive a Registration ID and Password.
4.  **Login:** Use your credentials to log in to the application portal.
5.  **Fill Application Form:** Accurately fill in all required personal, educational, and contact details in the online application form.
6.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other essential documents (e.g., mark sheets, certificates) in the prescribed format and size.
7.  **Pay Application Fee:** Pay the required examination fee online using available payment options.
8.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the information entered before final submission.
9.  **Print Application:** Download and print a copy of the submitted application form and the e-receipt of the fee payment for your records.

#### Important Links

| Link Type                  | URL                                   |
| :------------------------- | :------------------------------------ |
| Official Notification (PDF)| To Be Released                        |
| Apply Online               | To Be Activated                       |
| Official BPSC Website      | `https://bpsc.bih.nic.in/` (Expected) |

content:
# Indian Navy B.Tech Entry (10+2) Recruitment 2026 - Jan 2027 Batch

The Indian Navy is set to announce the B.Tech Entry Scheme (10+2 Cadet Entry Scheme) for the January 2027 batch. This prestigious scheme offers a direct path for eligible candidates to join the Indian Navy as officers after completing a four-year B.Tech course at the Indian Naval Academy, Ezhimala. Aspiring candidates are advised to keep an eye on the official Indian Navy recruitment website for the detailed notification and application schedule.

## Important Dates

The application process for the January 2027 batch is anticipated to commence in 2026. Exact dates will be notified with the release of the official advertisement.

| Event                           | Date                   |
| :------------------------------ | :--------------------- |
| Notification Release Date       | To be announced (2026) |
| Online Application Start Date   | To be announced (2026) |
| Online Application End Date     | To be announced (2026) |
| SSB Interview Dates             | To be announced        |
| Merit List / Final Result       | To be announced        |

## Application Fee

Based on previous recruitments for the B.Tech Entry Scheme, typically there is no application fee for candidates. However, applicants must refer to the official notification for the Jan 2027 batch for confirmation.

| Category | Fee (INR) |
| :------- | :-------- |
| General  | N/A       |
| OBC      | N/A       |
| SC/ST    | N/A       |
| All Females | N/A       |

## Vacancy Details

The total number of vacancies and their distribution across different branches (Executive, Education, Technical) will be specified in the official notification. Candidates will be selected based on their JEE (Main) 2026 All India Rank and subsequent SSB Interview performance.

## Eligibility Criteria

### Educational Qualification

*   Candidates must have passed the 10+2 examination or its equivalent from any Board recognized by the Central/State Government with at least 70% aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM) and at least 50% marks in English (either in Class X or Class XII).
*   Candidates must have appeared for the JEE (Main) 2026 examination.

### Age Limit

*   Candidates should be born between specific dates as mentioned in the official notification for the January 2027 batch. Typically, the age limit ranges from 16.5 to 19.5 years on the date of joining.

### Marital Status

*   Only unmarried male and female candidates are eligible.

### Physical and Medical Standards

*   Candidates must meet the prescribed physical and medical standards as laid down by the Indian Navy. Details regarding height, weight, vision, etc., will be available in the official notification.

## Selection Process

The selection process for the Indian Navy B.Tech Entry Scheme typically involves the following stages:

1.  **Shortlisting of Applications:** Applications will be shortlisted based on the JEE (Main) 2026 All India Rank (AIR).
2.  **SSB Interview:** Shortlisted candidates will be called for an SSB (Services Selection Board) interview, which lasts for five days and comprises two stages:
    *   **Stage I:** Intelligence Test, Picture Perception and Discussion Test (PP&DT).
    *   **Stage II:** Psychological Tests, Group Testing Officer (GTO) Tasks, Personal Interview, and Conference.
3.  **Medical Examination:** Candidates who qualify in the SSB interview will undergo a comprehensive medical examination.
4.  **Merit List:** A final merit list will be prepared based on the candidate's performance in the SSB interview. JEE (Main) scores are only for shortlisting and do not contribute to the final merit.

## How to Apply

Candidates interested in joining the Indian Navy through the B.Tech Entry Scheme (10+2) can apply online by following these steps (details subject to official notification):

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official recruitment website of the Indian Navy (www.joinindiannavy.gov.in).
2.  **Register:** If you are a new user, click on 'Register' and create an account using your email ID and mobile number.
3.  **Log In:** Log in to your account using the registered credentials.
4.  **Fill Application Form:** Navigate to the 'Current Opportunities' section and select the 'B.Tech Entry (10+2)' application link. Fill in all personal, educational, and JEE (Main) 2026 details accurately.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of required documents, including your photograph, signature, 10th and 12th class mark sheets, and JEE (Main) 2026 score card, as per the specified format and size.
6.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the information entered in the application form before final submission.
7.  **Print Application:** After successful submission, take a printout of the application form for your records.

Candidates are advised to regularly check the official Indian Navy website for the latest updates regarding the notification release and application process.

content:
# CTET September 2026 Online Application: Your Path to a Teaching Career

The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) invites applications for the Central Teacher Eligibility Test (CTET) September 2026. This examination is crucial for candidates aspiring to become teachers for Classes 1 to 8 in central government schools like KVS, NVS, Central Tibetan Schools, and others, as well as schools under the administrative control of Union Territories and private schools.

## Important Dates & Schedules

Stay updated with the key timelines for the CTET September 2026 application process:

| Event                        | Date                 |
| :--------------------------- | :------------------- |
| Application Start Date       | To be announced      |
| Application End Date         | To be announced      |
| Last Date for Fee Payment    | To be announced      |
| Correction Window            | To be announced      |
| **Exam Date (September 2026)** | **September 2026** |
| Result Declaration           | To be announced      |

## Application Fee Details

The application fee varies based on the category of the applicant and the number of papers opted for. Payment can be made online through various modes.

| Category                     | For Paper I or Paper II | For Both Papers I & II |
| :--------------------------- | :---------------------- | :--------------------- |
| General / OBC                | ₹1000/-                 | ₹1200/-                |
| SC / ST / Differently Abled  | ₹500/-                  | ₹600/-                 |

*Payment Mode: Online (Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking)*

## Eligibility Criteria (Post Qualifications)

Candidates must meet the specific educational qualifications for each paper to be eligible for CTET September 2026.

### For Class I-V (Paper I)

*   Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education (by whatever name known).
*   **OR** Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education, in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure), Regulations, 2002.
*   **OR** Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 4-year Bachelor of Elementary Education (B.El.Ed).
*   **OR** Graduation with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in 1-year Bachelor in Education (B.Ed).
*   **OR** Graduation with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in 1-year Bachelor in Education (B.Ed), in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure) Regulations issued from time to time in this regard.
*   **OR** Post-Graduation with a minimum of 55% marks or equivalent grade and passed or appearing in final year of a three-year integrated B.Ed.-M.Ed.

### For Class VI-VIII (Paper II)

*   Graduation and passed or appearing in final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education (by whatever name known).
*   **OR** Graduation with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in 1-year Bachelor in Education (B.Ed).
*   **OR** Graduation with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in 1-year Bachelor in Education (B.Ed), in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure) Regulations issued from time to time in this regard.
*   **OR** Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 4-year Bachelor in Elementary Education (B.El.Ed).
*   **OR** Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 4-year B.A./B.Sc.Ed or B.A.Ed/B.Sc.Ed.
*   **OR** Graduation with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in 1-year B.Ed. (Special Education).
*   **OR** Post-Graduation with a minimum of 55% marks or equivalent grade and passed or appearing in final year of a three-year integrated B.Ed.-M.Ed.

*Note: Candidates who are appearing in the final year of a B.Ed. or D.El.Ed. course are also eligible to apply.*

## Age Limit

There is no upper age limit for applying to the CTET examination.

## How to Apply

Follow these simple steps to successfully submit your CTET September 2026 online application:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official CTET website: `https://ctet.nic.in`.
2.  **Access Application Link:** Click on the 'Apply Online' link for CTET September 2026.
3.  **Registration:** Register by providing basic details such as your name, parent's name, date of birth, and email ID. A provisional registration number and password will be generated.
4.  **Login and Form Filling:** Log in using your registration number and password. Fill out the detailed application form with personal, educational, and communication details.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-sized photograph and signature in the specified format and size.
6.  **Pay Application Fee:** Proceed to pay the application fee online through Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking.
7.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the information entered in the form before final submission to avoid any errors.
8.  **Print Confirmation Page:** After successful submission and payment, download and print the confirmation page for your records.

## Important Links

| Link Type         | URL                         |
| :---------------- | :-------------------------- |
| Official Website  | `https://ctet.nic.in`       |
| Apply Online      | `https://ctet.nic.in`       |

content:
## SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026: 7150 Vacancies Announced

The State Bank of India (SBI) has released a notification for the recruitment of Apprentices, offering a fantastic opportunity for young graduates across India. A total of 7150 vacancies have been announced, making this one of the most significant apprenticeship drives in the banking sector. This program aims to provide hands-on training and exposure to banking operations, preparing candidates for future roles in the financial industry.

### Vacancy Details

| Post Name   | Total Vacancies |
| :---------- | :-------------- |
| Apprentice  | 7150            |

### Eligibility Criteria

Candidates applying for the SBI Apprentice positions must meet the following eligibility requirements:

*   **Educational Qualification:** A Bachelor's degree in any discipline from a recognized University or an institution recognized by the Central Government. Specific details regarding minimum percentage or other qualifications will be mentioned in the detailed notification.
*   **Local Language Proficiency:** Candidates must be proficient in the specified local language of the State/UT from which they are applying. This proficiency will be assessed through a language test.

### Age Limit

*   **Minimum Age:** 20 years
*   **Maximum Age:** 28 years

(Age will be reckoned as on a specific cut-off date to be announced in the official notification). Age relaxation for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD) will be applicable as per Government of India rules.

### Application Fee

Applicants are required to pay a non-refundable application fee. The fee structure is expected to be as follows, subject to change in the official notification:

| Category               | Application Fee |
| :--------------------- | :-------------- |
| General/OBC/EWS        | Rs. 300/-       |
| SC/ST/PwBD             | NIL             |

Payment can be made online using Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking.

### Important Dates

The exact dates for the application process and examination will be released with the detailed official notification. Candidates are advised to regularly check the SBI careers portal for updates.

| Event                          | Date               |
| :----------------------------- | :----------------- |
| Online Application Start Date  | To be Announced    |
| Online Application End Date    | To be Announced    |
| Last Date for Fee Payment      | To be Announced    |
| Online Exam Tentative Date     | To be Announced    |

### Selection Process

The selection process for SBI Apprentice Recruitment typically involves the following stages:

1.  **Online Written Test:** An objective type test comprising sections like General/Financial Awareness, General English, Quantitative Aptitude, and Reasoning Ability.
2.  **Test of Local Language:** Candidates will have to qualify a test of the specified opted local language.
3.  **Medical Examination:** Final selection will be subject to clearing a medical examination.

### How to Apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Follow these steps to apply online for the SBI Apprentice positions:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the careers section of the State Bank of India website: `https://bank.sbi/web/careers/current-openings`.
2.  **Find the Notification:** Look for the 'Recruitment of Apprentices' notification for the 2026 cycle under 'Current Openings'.
3.  **New Registration:** Click on 'New Registration' if you are a first-time applicant. Provide basic details like name, contact number, and email ID to generate a provisional registration number and password.
4.  **Fill Application Form:** Log in using your registration credentials and fill in the detailed application form with all required personal, educational, and communication details.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration as per the specifications mentioned in the notification.
6.  **Pay Application Fee:** Proceed to pay the application fee online through the available payment gateway.
7.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the entered information before final submission. No changes will be allowed after final submission.
8.  **Print Application:** After successful submission and payment, take a printout of the e-receipt and the submitted application form for future reference.

### Important Links

*   **Official Website:** [https://bank.sbi/web/careers](https://bank.sbi/web/careers)

content:
### India Post Office GDS Result 2026 (1st Merit List) Released

The India Post Office has officially declared the 1st Merit List for the Gramin Dak Sevak (GDS) recruitment cycle of 2026. This is a crucial announcement for all candidates who participated in the selection process. The merit list is now available for download on the official India Post GDS online portal.

This provisional merit list contains the names and registration details of candidates who have been shortlisted based on their academic performance and other selection criteria. Candidates whose names appear in this list will be called for the subsequent Document Verification (DV) stage. It is imperative for all candidates to verify their status and prepare for the next phase of the recruitment process.

#### Important Dates

Candidates are advised to keep track of the following crucial dates related to the GDS 2026 1st Merit List:

| Event | Date (Tentative) |
| :------------------------------ | :---------------- |
| GDS Application Period | To Be Announced (Previous cycle) |
| Last Date for Application | To Be Announced (Previous cycle) |
| **GDS Result (1st Merit List) Release** | **Expected: January 2026** |
| Document Verification | To Be Announced Shortly after Result |

*Note: The dates mentioned are indicative based on typical recruitment cycles. Candidates must refer to the official website for precise and updated schedules.*

#### Steps to Download Your GDS Result (1st Merit List)

Follow these simple steps to check and download your India Post Office GDS Result 2026 (1st Merit List):

1.  **Visit the Official Website**: Go to the official India Post GDS online portal at [indiapostgdsonline.gov.in](https://indiapostgdsonline.gov.in/).
2.  **Navigate to Results Section**: On the homepage, look for the 'Candidate's Corner' or 'Results' section.
3.  **Select Your Circle/State**: You will typically find a list of Circles (States). Click on the link corresponding to the State/Circle you applied for.
4.  **Find the Merit List**: Locate the link for "List of Shortlisted Candidates" or "1st Merit List" for the GDS 2026 cycle.
5.  **Download PDF**: Click on the relevant link to download the merit list PDF file.
6.  **Search Your Details**: Open the downloaded PDF and use the search function (Ctrl+F) to find your Registration Number or Name to check if you have been shortlisted.
7.  **Save for Future Reference**: It is highly recommended to download and save a copy of the merit list for your records.

#### Required Documents for Document Verification

Shortlisted candidates will be required to appear for Document Verification (DV). It is essential to have the following original documents along with self-attested copies:

*   Class 10th (Matriculation) Mark Sheet and Certificate
*   Computer Certificate (if required for the post)
*   Community/Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS)
*   Disability Certificate (if applicable)
*   Date of Birth Proof (if not available on 10th certificate)
*   Medical Certificate
*   Proof of Residence
*   Passport-size photographs
*   Any other relevant documents as specified in the official notification.

Candidates are advised to bring all original documents and two sets of self-attested photocopies for the verification process.

#### Important Links

| Description | Link |
| :----------------------------------------- | :--------------------------------------------- |
| **Download GDS Result 2026 (1st Merit List)** | [Click Here (Link will be active on official website)](https://indiapostgdsonline.gov.in/) |
| Official India Post GDS Website | [https://indiapostgdsonline.gov.in/](https://indiapostgdsonline.gov.in/) |

## SSC CGL 2026 Recruitment Overview

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has announced the Combined Graduate Level Examination (CGL) 2026 to fill approximately 12,256 vacancies in Group B and Group C posts across various government departments. This is a golden opportunity for graduates seeking a career in central government services.

### Important Dates

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|

- Assistant Audit Officer / Assistant Accounts Officer
- Assistant Section Officer
- Inspector of Income Tax

- Tax Assistant / Postal Assistant / Senior Secretariat Assistant

### Eligibility Criteria

**Age Limit (as on 01.08.2026):** 18-30 years (varies by post). Relaxation as per government rules.

**Educational Qualification:** Bachelor's Degree from a recognized university. Additional qualifications for specific posts (see official notification).

### Selection Process

1. Tier-I Examination (Computer Based Test) – Qualifying
2. Tier-II Examination (Computer Based Test) – Merit
3. Document Verification

### How to Apply

A3. Bachelor's Degree from a recognized university.

A4. ₹25,500 – ₹1,51,100 per month (Pay Level-4 to Pay Level-8).

A5. Yes, 0.50 marks for each wrong answer in Tier-I and Tier-II.

content:
## UPSC NDA NA II Recruitment 2026 – Apply Online for 394 Posts

The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has officially released the UPSC NDA & NA Examination II 2026 notification (Examination Notice No. 10/2026-NDA-II) for admission to the National Defence Academy (NDA) and Naval Academy (10+2 Cadet Entry Scheme). This is a golden opportunity for unmarried male and female candidates to join the Indian Army, Navy and Air Force as Commissioned Officers.

### Application Fee

| Category | Fee |
|----------|-----|
| General / OBC / EWS | ₹100 |
| Female / SC / ST / Wards of JCOs/NCOs/ORs | Exempted (No fee) |

Fee can be paid online through Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card or UPI.

### Important Dates

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Notification Release Date | 20 May 2026 |
| Start Date for Online Application | 20 May 2026 |
| Last Date to Apply Online | 09 June 2026 |
| Date of Written Examination | 13 September 2026 |
| Result of Written Examination (Tentative) | October 2026 |

### Vacancy Details

| Academy | Vacancies |
|---------|-----------|
| NDA Army | 208 |
| NDA Navy | 42 |
| NDA Air Force | 120 |
| Naval Academy | 24 |
| **Total** | **394** (Male-370, Female-24) |

### Eligibility Criteria

- **Age Limit:** Only unmarried male and female candidates born not earlier than 02 January 2008 and not later than 01 January 2011 are eligible.
- **Educational Qualification:**
  - Army Wing of NDA: 12th Class pass of the 10+2 pattern or equivalent.
  - Air Force, Naval Wings of NDA & Naval Academy (10+2 Cadet Entry): 12th Class pass with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics.
  - Candidates appearing in 12th standard can also apply provided they can submit proof of passing by the prescribed date.

### Salary & Benefits

During training at NDA, cadets receive a fixed stipend of ₹56,100 per month (as per 7th CPC). After successful completion of training and commissioning as Lieutenant (Army), Sub-Lieutenant (Navy) or Flying Officer (Air Force), candidates are placed in Pay Level-10 (₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500) + Military Service Pay + Dearness Allowance, House Rent Allowance, Transport Allowance, Ration, Medical facilities, Canteen facilities, and other defence perks.

### Selection Process

1. Written Examination (Objective Type)
2. SSB Interview (Intelligence & Personality Test)
3. Medical Examination
4. Document Verification

### How to Apply

1. Visit the official UPSC website https://upsconline.nic.in.
2. Register and create your One-Time Registration (OTR) profile.
3. Login and fill the Common Application Form (CAF).
4. Upload live photograph, signature (three times on plain paper) and required documents.
5. Pay the application fee (if applicable).
6. Submit the form and take a printout for future reference.

### Important Links

- **Official Notification PDF:** [Download PDF](#)
- **Apply Online:** [Apply Online](https://upsconline.nic.in)
- **UPSC Official Website:** [Visit UPSC Website](https://upsc.gov.in)

### FAQs

**Q1. How many vacancies are announced in UPSC NDA II 2026?**
A1. Total 394 vacancies (370 for Male and 24 for Female candidates).

**Q2. What is the last date to apply for NDA II 2026?**
A2. The last date to apply online is 09 June 2026.

**Q3. What is the age limit for UPSC NDA 2026?**
A3. Candidates must be born between 02 January 2008 and 01 January 2011 (unmarried only).

**Q4. What is the educational qualification required for NDA II 2026?**
A4. 12th Class Pass. Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics are compulsory for Air Force, Naval Wings and Naval Academy.

**Q5. What is the selection process for UPSC NDA II 2026?**
A5. Written Examination → SSB Interview → Medical Examination → Document Verification.

**Q6. Is there any application fee relaxation for female candidates?**
A6. Yes, female candidates are fully exempted from payment of application fee.

A1. Total 451 vacancies (IMA-100, INA-26, AFA-32, OTA Men-275, OTA Women-18).

**Q2. What is the last date to apply for CDS II 2026?**
A2. The last date to apply online is 09 June 2026.
