A3. Age limit is 19-24 years for IMA/INA, 20-24 years for AFA, and 19-25 years for OTA (varies as per academy).

**Q4. What is the educational qualification required for CDS 2026?**
A4. Bachelor’s Degree from a recognised University (specific requirements for Naval & Air Force Academy).

**Q5. What is the selection process for UPSC CDS II 2026?**
A5. Written Examination → SSB Interview → Document Verification → Medical Examination.

**Q6. Is there any application fee relaxation for female candidates?**
A6. Yes, female candidates are exempted from payment of application fee.

2. Go to the Careers / Recruitment section.
3. Click on the online application link and register with valid details.
4. Fill the application form carefully and upload all required supporting documents.
5. Pay the application fee online (if applicable).
6. Submit the form on or before 01 June 2026 (17:00 hrs IST).
7. Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.

### Important Links
- **Official Website**: [NABI](https://nabi.res.in/)
- **Notification PDF**: Not available
- **Apply Online**: [NABI Careers](https://nabi.res.in/)

### FAQs
**Q: What is the total number of vacancies?**
A: 01 Scientist-D post (EWS category).

**Q: What is the age limit?**
A: Maximum 50 years (as on closing date).

**Q: What is the pay scale?**
A: Pay Level-12 (₹78,800 – ₹2,09,200).

**Q: What is the selection process?**
A: Screening of applications + Personal Interview.

**Q: What is the application fee?**
A: EWS: ₹590 | EWS Women: ₹354 | OCI Abroad: USD 30/20.

**Q: What is the last date to apply?**
A: 01 June 2026 (17:00 hrs IST).

content:
## DSSSB 03/2026 Notification Out for 1,979 Posts

The Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) has released the official notification for recruitment under advertisement number 03/2026. This recruitment drive aims to fill 1,979 vacancies for various posts including Teacher, Junior Scientific Assistant, Sub-Inspector (SI), and others. The online application process will commence from 16 June 2026.

### Application Fee

| Category | Fee Amount |
|----------|------------|
| General / OBC | ₹100 |
| SC / ST / PwD / Female | ₹0 (Exempted) |

### Important Dates

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Start of Online Application | 16 June 2026 |
| Last Date to Apply | 15 July 2026 |
| Last Date for Fee Payment | 15 July 2026 |
| Exam Date (Tentative) | August/September 2026 |

### Vacancy Details

| Post Name | Number of Vacancies |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Teacher (Primary) | 800 |
| Teacher (Secondary) | 500 |
| Junior Scientific Assistant | 200 |
| Sub-Inspector (SI) | 300 |
| Other Posts | 179 |
| **Total** | **1,979** |

### Eligibility Criteria

- **Educational Qualification**: Candidates must have the required qualification as per the post. For Teacher posts, B.Ed. and CTET/STET qualification may be required. For Junior Scientific Assistant, a Bachelor's degree in Science. For SI, graduation degree.
- **Age Limit**: Minimum 18 years and maximum 30 years (age relaxation as per government norms).

### How to Apply

1. Visit the official DSSSB website: [dsssb.delhi.gov.in](https://dsssb.delhi.gov.in).
2. Click on the 'Apply Online' link for advertisement 03/2026.
3. Register with a valid email and mobile number.
4. Fill in the application form with personal, educational, and other details.
5. Upload scanned photograph, signature, and required documents.
6. Pay the application fee online.
7. Submit the form and take a printout for future reference.

### Selection Process

The selection will be based on a written examination followed by skill test/typing test (if applicable) and document verification.

For more details, candidates are advised to read the official notification carefully.

## Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Notification

The Indian Air Force has released the official notification for AFCAT 02/2026, inviting applications for 379 vacancies in Flying, Ground Duty (Technical and Non-Technical) branches. This is a prestigious opportunity for unmarried male and female candidates to join the Indian Air Force as commissioned officers.

### Application Fee
| Category | Fee |
|----------|-----|
| General / OBC / EWS | ₹550 |
| SC / ST | Nil |
| All Female Candidates | Nil |

### Important Dates
| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Start of Online Application | 1st June 2025 |
| Last Date to Apply | 30th June 2025 |
| AFCAT Exam Date | 9th August 2025 |
| AFSB Interview | To be announced |

### Vacancy Details
| Branch | Vacancies |
|--------|-----------|
| Flying | 150 |
| Ground Duty (Technical) | 120 |
| Ground Duty (Non-Technical) | 109 |
| Total | 379 |

### Eligibility Criteria
- **Educational Qualification**: Bachelor's degree in any discipline for Flying and Ground Duty Non-Technical; B.E./B.Tech or equivalent for Ground Duty Technical. Final year students can also apply.
- **Age Limit**: 20 to 24 years for Flying branch (as of 1st July 2026); 20 to 26 years for Ground Duty branches.
- **Physical Standards**: As per IAF norms.

### Selection Process
1. **AFCAT Online Exam**: Objective type, 100 questions, 2 hours duration.
2. **ASPB Interview**: For shortlisted candidates.
3. **Medical Examination**: As per IAF standards.

### How to Apply
1. Visit the official Indian Air Force recruitment website at https://afcat.cdac.in.
2. Click on 'AFCAT 02/2026' and register with a valid email and mobile number.
3. Fill in the online application form with personal, educational, and communication details.
4. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and required documents.
5. Pay the application fee (if applicable) via online mode.
6. Submit the form and take a printout for future reference.

### Important Links
- **Apply Online**: [https://afcat.cdac.in](https://afcat.cdac.in)
- **Official Notification**: Not available yet.

Sainik School Goalpara, a residential public school run by Sainik Schools Society under Ministry of Defence, Govt. of India, has released Advertisement No. SSG/02/2026 dated 20 May 2026 for recruitment of 13 Coaches & Club Teachers on contractual basis for Academic Session 2026-27.

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|

| Walk-in-Interview (Football, Volleyball, Basketball, Swimming) | 18 June 2026 (09:00 AM) |
| Walk-in-Interview (Boxing, Hockey, Skating, Karate, Dance, Music) | 19 June 2026 (09:00 AM) |

### Vacancy Details
| S.No. | Post | Vacancies | Gender | Walk-in Date |
|-------|------|-----------|--------|--------------|
| 1 | Football Coach | 02 | Male-01, Female-01 | 18 Jun 2026 |

| 8 | Karate Coach | 01 | Open (Male/Female) | 19 Jun 2026 |
| 9 | Dance Teacher | 01 | Open (Male/Female) | 19 Jun 2026 |
| 10 | Music Teacher | 01 | Open (Male/Female) | 19 Jun 2026 |

### Remuneration & Benefits
- Football / Volleyball / Basketball / Boxing / Hockey Coach: ₹20,000/- per month
- Swimming Coach cum Life Guard: ₹30,000/- per month
- Skating / Karate / Dance / Music Teacher: ₹17,000/- per month
- Additional Benefits: Free Accommodation & Meals in Cadets’ Mess.
- Contractual appointment for Academic Session 2026-27 (extendable as per school requirement).

### Eligibility Criteria
**Age Limit (as on 01.06.2026):**
- Maximum 35 years for most coaching posts
- No age bar for Skating Coach, Karate Coach, Dance Teacher & Music Teacher

**Educational & Professional Qualification:**
- Football / Volleyball / Basketball / Swimming / Boxing / Hockey Coach: NIS Coach OR Diploma/Certificate in respective game OR National Level Player representation

- Personal Interview

- TA/DA is not admissible.

1. Download the detailed notification and prepare your resume as per the prescribed format.
2. Carry one recent passport size photograph.
3. Bring all original documents along with one set of self-attested photocopies (mark sheets, certificates, experience letters, Aadhaar Card, etc.).
4. Report at the venue on the respective date and time mentioned above.
5. Venue: Sainik School Goalpara, PO: Rajapara, Dist: Goalpara, Assam – 783133.
6. Be ready for Skill Test / Demonstration and Interview on the same day.
7. Candidates should be prepared to stay for an extra day if required (own arrangement).

A1. Total 13 vacancies have been announced for various Coaches and Club Teachers.

A2. 18 June 2026 (Football, Volleyball, Basketball, Swimming) and 19 June 2026 (Boxing, Hockey, Skating, Karate, Dance, Music) at 09:00 AM.

A3. NIS Coach / Diploma / Certificate in respective game OR National Level representation (specific to each post).

A4. ₹17,000 to ₹30,000 per month (consolidated) + Free Accommodation & Meals in Cadets’ Mess.

A5. Skill Test / Demonstration + Personal Interview.

A6. No application fee is required for this walk-in recruitment.

| Gujarat Metro Rail Helper, Technician, Operator & Other | 132 | 12/06/2026 |
| Konkan Railway Junior Engineer | 4 | 05/06/2026 |

### Eligibility Criteria
- **Educational Qualification:** Minimum 10th pass for Group D; ITI for Group C & D; Graduate/Postgraduate for Group A & B.
- **Age Limit:** 18 to 30 years (age relaxation as per government norms).

### Selection Process
1. Written Examination (CBT)
2. Skill Test / Aptitude Test (for specific posts)
3. Document Verification
4. Medical Examination

### How to Apply
1. Visit the official RRB website: [www.rrbcdg.gov.in](https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in)
2. Click on the 'Apply Online' link for the desired post.
3. Register and fill the application form.
4. Upload required documents and pay fee.
5. Submit and print confirmation.

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: What are the jobs in the Railway Sector?**
A: Indian Railway jobs include Gazetted (Group 'A' and 'B'), Non-Gazetted (Group 'C' and 'D'), NTPC, Junior Engineer, RPF, RPSF, Act Apprentices, Level 1, and Level 2 posts.

**Q: How to apply for railway recruitment?**
A: Visit the official RRB website or respective zonal railway websites for detailed notifications and application links.

**Q: What are the qualifications for railway jobs?**
A: Minimum 10th pass or ITI for Group C & D; Graduate/Postgraduate for Group A & B.

**Q: How many railway zones are there in India?**
A: There are 21 Railway Zones.

**Q: Which is the best job in the Railway?**
A: It depends on qualifications and preferences. Popular posts include Clerk, Station Master, Ticket Collector for 10th/12th pass, and Group A Engineering Services for engineers.

**Q: How many jobs are there in the Indian Railways?**
A: Each year, more than one lakh vacancies open up.

**Q: Is a career in the railway possible after 12th?**
A: Yes, 10th or 12th pass candidates can apply for Group C and D posts.

**Q: Who is eligible to apply for railway jobs?**
A: Indian citizens who have passed at least Matriculation/10th standard are eligible.

**Q: Why does www.indgovtjobs.in have a Railway Jobs list page?**
A: The page provides weekly updates for job seekers looking for railway jobs and offers free alerts.

### Important Links
- Official RRB Website: [https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in](https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in)
- Apply Online: [https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in](https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in)

*Note: Candidates are advised to regularly check the official website for updates and notifications.*

title: DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026
department: Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) Recruitment & Assessment Centre (RAC)
overview: The Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO), through its Recruitment & Assessment Centre (RAC), has announced a significant recruitment drive for 33 Scientist posts (Scientist ‘E’, ‘D’ & ‘C’) within the DRDS cadre. This is an exceptional opportunity for accomplished engineers, scientists, and researchers to contribute to India's premier defence R&D sector under the Ministry of Defence. Interested and eligible candidates are invited to apply online through the official RAC portal, with the application window open from June 06, 2026, until June 30, 2026.
eligibility: Candidates must possess a First Class BE/B.Tech/M.Sc/M.Pharm degree in relevant disciplines with varying years of experience: 11 years for Scientist ‘E’, 7 years for Scientist ‘D’, and 3 years for Scientist ‘C’. Detailed discipline-wise essential qualifications are specified in the official notification.
age_limit: For Scientist ‘E’ and ‘D’, the maximum age is 45 years. For Scientist ‘C’, the maximum age is 35 years. Age relaxations are applicable as per government norms: up to 10 years for PwD candidates, up to 5 years for Central Govt. employees, and for Ex-Servicemen as per rules, with the maximum age not exceeding 56 years including all relaxations. Age limits are calculated as on the closing date of advertisement.
salary: Selected candidates will receive salaries as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix: Scientist ‘E’ (Level-13) will receive ₹1,23,100 – ₹2,15,900; Scientist ‘D’ (Level-12) will receive ₹78,800 – ₹2,09,200; and Scientist ‘C’ (Level-11) will receive ₹67,700 – ₹2,08,700. In addition to the basic pay, selected individuals will be entitled to Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance (TA), Medical facilities, National Pension System (NPS), Leave Travel Concession (LTC), and other Central Government perks, along with excellent career growth under the Flexible Complementing Scheme (FCS).
how_to_apply:
Prospective candidates must apply online through the official Recruitment & Assessment Centre (RAC) portal. Follow these steps:
1.  Visit the official RAC website: rac.gov.in.
2.  Navigate to the 'Advertisements' or 'Apply Online' section for Advertisement No. 157.
3.  Click on 'Apply Online' for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026.
4.  Register by providing basic details if you are a new user.
5.  Log in using your registration credentials.
6.  Fill out the online application form carefully with all required personal and educational details.
7.  Upload scanned copies of necessary documents, photographs, and signatures as per the specified format and size.
8.  Pay the application fee online, if applicable, through the provided payment gateway. SC/ST/PwD/Women candidates are exempted from fee payment.
9.  Review the entire application form for any errors before final submission.
10. Submit the application and take a printout of the submitted form for future reference.
meta_title: DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 2026: Online Form, Eligibility & Benefits
meta_description: The Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO), through its Recruitment & Assessment Centre (RAC), has announced a significant recruitment drive fo
content:
## DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026: A Golden Opportunity for Engineers & Scientists

The Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO), through its Recruitment & Assessment Centre (RAC), has released Advertisement No. 157 for the lateral recruitment of 33 highly skilled Scientists. This significant recruitment drive aims to fill positions for Scientist ‘E’, ‘D’, and ‘C’ in various critical disciplines within the DRDS cadre. This presents an unparalleled opportunity for experienced and qualified engineers, scientists, and researchers to join India's premier defence R&D organization, operating under the Ministry of Defence, and contribute directly to national security and technological advancement. The online application process commenced on June 06, 2026, and the extended deadline for submission is June 30, 2026. Eligible male and female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply through the official RAC portal at rac.gov.in.

### DRDO RAC Scientist Vacancy Details 2026

| Post Discipline                                                     | Vacancies | Experience Required |
| :------------------------------------------------------------------ | :-------- | :------------------ |
| **Scientist ‘E’**                                                   |           |                     |
| Electronics & Communication Engg. / Mechanical / Production / Aerospace Engg. | 02        | 11 Years            |
| **Scientist ‘D’**                                                   |           |                     |
| Computer Science & Engg., Aeronautical/Aerospace, Mechanical, Physics/Photonics/Optics, Chemical, Electronics & Telecommunication, Physics/Radiation Physics | 11        | 07 Years            |
| **Scientist ‘C’**                                                   |           |                     |
| Electronics & Communication, Computer Science & Engg., Oceanography, Mathematics/Applied Mathematics, Physiology, Electrical, Pharmacology, Remote Sensing/GIS/Civil, Medical Physics/Radiological Physics, Electronics & Communication / Mechanical | 20        | 03 Years            |
| **Grand Total**                                                     | **33**    |                     |

### DRDO RAC Scientist 2026 Pay Scale & Benefits

Selected candidates will be offered an attractive remuneration package as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix and Central Government rules:

*   **Scientist ‘E’ (Level-13):** ₹1,23,100 – ₹2,15,900
*   **Scientist ‘D’ (Level-12):** ₹78,800 – ₹2,09,200
*   **Scientist ‘C’ (Level-11):** ₹67,700 – ₹2,08,700

In addition to the competitive basic pay, appointees will receive various allowances including Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance (TA), comprehensive Medical facilities, National Pension System (NPS), Leave Travel Concession (LTC), and other benefits applicable to Central Government employees. The Flexible Complementing Scheme (FCS) ensures excellent career growth prospects within the organization.

### DRDO RAC Scientist 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet the following eligibility requirements as on the closing date of the advertisement:

#### Age Limit

*   **Scientist ‘E’ / ‘D’:** Maximum 45 years
*   **Scientist ‘C’:** Maximum 35 years

**Age Relaxation:**
Applicable as per Government of India rules:
*   Up to 10 years for Persons with Disabilities (PwD)
*   Up to 5 years for Central Government employees
*   For Ex-Servicemen as per extant rules
*   The maximum age, including all relaxations, shall not exceed 56 years.

#### Educational Qualification & Experience

Applicants must possess a First Class degree in the relevant engineering or science discipline from a recognized university/institution, along with the specified years of experience:

*   **Scientist ‘E’:** First Class BE/B.Tech or equivalent + 11 years of relevant experience.
*   **Scientist ‘D’:** First Class BE/B.Tech / M.Sc or equivalent + 7 years of relevant experience.
*   **Scientist ‘C’:** First Class BE/B.Tech / M.Sc / M.Pharm or equivalent + 3 years of relevant experience.

Candidates are advised to refer to the official notification for complete discipline-wise essential qualifications and experience details.

### DRDO RAC Scientist 2026 Selection Process

The selection for DRDO RAC Scientist posts will involve a rigorous process designed to identify the most suitable candidates:

1.  **Screening of Applications:** Initial screening will be conducted based on administrative and technical criteria to shortlist eligible candidates.
2.  **Shortlisting for Interview:** Candidates who successfully clear the screening stage will be shortlisted for the Personal Interview round.
3.  **Final Personal Interview:** The interview will be conducted in-person only at RAC, Delhi.
4.  **Final Selection:** The final selection will be purely based on the merit achieved in the Personal Interview. A minimum of 75% marks (i.e., 75 out of 100) is required in the interview for selection.

### Application Fee for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026

| Category                        | Fee    |
| :------------------------------ | :----- |
| General / OBC / EWS (Male)      | ₹100/- |
| SC / ST / PwD / Women           | Nil    |

*   Fees are payable online through the official portal.

### Important Dates for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026

| Event                       | Date           |
| :-------------------------- | :------------- |
| Online Application Start Date | 06 June 2026   |
| Last Date for Online Registration | 30 June 2026 (16:00 hours) |
| Date of Personal Interview  | To be intimated through RAC website |

### How to Apply for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026

Interested and eligible candidates must apply online by following these steps:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official Recruitment & Assessment Centre (RAC) portal at **rac.gov.in**.
2.  **Locate the Advertisement:** Find the 'Advertisements' or 'Apply Online' section and look for Advertisement No. 157 for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026.
3.  **New Registration:** If you are a first-time applicant, click on the 'New Registration' link and provide the necessary basic details to create your login credentials.
4.  **Login:** Use your registered email ID and password to log in to the application portal.
5.  **Fill Application Form:** Carefully fill in all mandatory fields in the online application form, including personal information, educational qualifications, and experience details.
6.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph, signature, educational certificates, experience certificates, and any other required documents as per the specifications mentioned in the official notification.
7.  **Pay Application Fee:** If you belong to the General, OBC, or EWS (Male) category, proceed to pay the ₹100/- application fee online using net banking, credit card, or debit card. SC/ST/PwD/Women candidates are exempted from fee payment.
8.  **Review and Submit:** Thoroughly review all the information entered in the application form before final submission to ensure accuracy and completeness.
9.  **Print Application:** After successful submission, download and print a copy of your submitted application form for your records.

### Important Links

*   **Apply Online:** [https://rac.gov.in](https://rac.gov.in)

content:
## HSSC CET Group D Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for Various Posts

The Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) has officially announced the upcoming Common Eligibility Test (CET) for Group D posts in 2026. This recruitment drive is a golden opportunity for candidates aspiring to secure Group D positions across various departments, boards, corporations, and universities under the Haryana Government. The CET serves as a qualifying examination, and candidates who clear it will be eligible for subsequent recruitment phases.

### Important Dates

| Event                          | Date                |
| :----------------------------- | :------------------ |
| Notification Release Date      | To be announced     |
| Online Application Start Date  | To be announced     |
| Online Application End Date    | To be announced     |
| Application Fee Last Date      | To be announced     |
| Admit Card Release Date        | To be announced     |
| CET Exam Date                  | To be announced     |
| Result Declaration             | To be announced     |

### Vacancy Details

Specific vacancy numbers for HSSC CET Group D Recruitment 2026 are yet to be released. The official notification will provide a detailed breakdown of posts available across different departments and categories. Candidates are advised to refer to the official notification once it is published for precise vacancy figures.

### Eligibility Criteria

#### A. Educational Qualification

Candidates must have passed 10th (Matriculation) or 12th (Intermediate) Grade from a recognized board or institution in India. Some specific Group D posts might have additional qualification requirements, which will be elaborated in the official notification.

#### B. Age Limit

The age limit for HSSC CET Group D Recruitment 2026 is generally between **18 to 42 years** as on the cut-off date mentioned in the official notification. Age relaxation rules will apply for candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC), Backward Classes (BC), Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Persons with Disabilities (PwD), and Ex-Servicemen categories as per Haryana Government norms.

### Application Fee

The application fee for HSSC CET Group D Recruitment 2026 will be as follows. Exact figures and payment methods will be confirmed in the detailed official notification.

| Category                                 | Fee (Tentative) |
| :--------------------------------------- | :-------------- |
| General / Other State Candidates         | To be announced |
| Haryana Residents (SC/BC/EWS)            | To be announced |
| Physically Handicapped / Ex-Servicemen   | To be announced |

*   Payment Mode: Online through Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card.

### Selection Process

The selection process for HSSC CET Group D Recruitment 2026 typically involves the following stages:

1.  **Common Eligibility Test (CET):** A written examination covering general awareness, reasoning, mathematics, science, English, Hindi, and Haryana-specific general knowledge.
2.  **Socio-Economic Criteria and Experience:** Marks may be awarded based on socio-economic criteria and experience as per HSSC guidelines.
3.  **Document Verification:** Shortlisted candidates will undergo verification of their original documents.
4.  **Medical Examination:** Final selection may be subject to a medical fitness test.

### How to Apply

Follow these step-by-step instructions to apply for HSSC CET Group D Recruitment 2026:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official website of the Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) - `hssc.gov.in` (link will be updated once active).
2.  **Find the Notification:** Look for the 'Recruitment' or 'Advertisement' section and find the notification for 'HSSC CET Group D Recruitment 2026'.
3.  **Read Official Notification:** Download and thoroughly read the official detailed notification to understand all eligibility criteria, important dates, and instructions.
4.  **New Registration:** If you are a new user, click on the 'New Registration' link and complete the registration process by providing basic details.
5.  **Fill Application Form:** Log in using your registration credentials and fill out the online application form with all required personal, educational, and contact details accurately.
6.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, educational certificates, and any other required documents in the prescribed format and size.
7.  **Pay Application Fee:** Proceed to pay the application fee through the available online payment modes (Net Banking, Credit/Debit Card).
8.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the entered information before final submission. Make sure there are no errors.
9.  **Print Application:** After successful submission, download and take a printout of the filled application form for your future reference.

### Important Links

| Link Title                        | URL                                |
| :-------------------------------- | :--------------------------------- |
| Official Notification (Will be Updated) | `null`                            |
| Apply Online (Will be Updated)    | `null`                            |
| HSSC Official Website             | `https://www.hssc.gov.in` (Tentative) |

meta_description: National Institute of Technology (NIT) Silchar has announced the Faculty Recruitment 2026 for Professor, Associate Professor, and Assistant Professor posit
content:
## NIT Silchar Faculty Recruitment 2026 – Apply Online for 35 Professor Posts

National Institute of Technology (NIT) Silchar invites online applications from Indian nationals for faculty positions including Professor, Associate Professor, and Assistant Professor (Grade-I & II). The recruitment drive aims to fill 35 Professor posts (9 fresh + 26 backlog) along with other faculty vacancies across various departments.

### Vacancy Details
| Post | Fresh Posts | Backlog Posts | Total |
|------|-------------|---------------|-------|
| Professor | 9 | 26 | 35 |

### Departments & Specializations
- **Civil Engineering**: Structural, Geotechnical, Transportation Engineering
- **Mechanical Engineering**: Robotics & Automation, Instrumentation & Control, Mechatronics, AI & Machine Learning
- **Electrical Engineering**: Microelectronics & VLSI, Communications & Signal Processing
- **Electronics & Communication Engineering**: VLSI, 5G/6G, Embedded Systems & IoT, MEMS/NEMS
- **Computer Science & Engineering**: Theoretical CS, Data Science & Big Data, Systems & Architecture, Cyber Security, Networks, HCI
- **Mathematics**: Stochastic Process/Financial Mathematics, Cryptography
- **Humanities & Social Sciences**: International Economics, English Language Teaching, Development Economics, Public Finance
- **Management Studies**: HRM, Finance, Operation & Logistics Management

### Eligibility Criteria
- Ph.D. in relevant discipline with consistently good academic record
- Relevant teaching and research experience as per post applied
- Publications in reputed journals and contribution to institutional development
- Detailed qualifications and credit point system in official notification

### Age Limit
As per Government of India / NIT rules.

### Salary / Pay Scale
| Post | Pay Level |
|------|-----------|
| Professor | Level-14A |
| Associate Professor | Level-13A2 |
| Assistant Professor Grade-I | Level-12 |
| Assistant Professor Grade-II | Level-10 |

### Application Fee
| Category | Fee |
|----------|-----|
| General / OBC (NCL) / EWS | ₹1,200 |
| SC / ST / PwD | ₹600 |

Payment Mode: Online through SBI SB Collect portal.

### Important Dates
| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Notification in Employment News | 20 June 2026 |
| Online Application Start | 21 May 2026 |
| Last Date for Online Application | 04 July 2026 |
| Last Date for Hard Copy Submission | Within 10 days after online closing |
| Cut-off Date for Eligibility | 21 May 2026 |

### Selection Process
Shortlisting based on academic record and credit points, followed by Interview.

### How to Apply
1. Visit the official online application portal: https://recruitment.nits.ac.in/ and register.
2. Fill the online application form carefully, select department and post, upload required documents.
3. Pay the application fee through SBI SB Collect portal.
4. Download and take a printout of the filled application form.
5. Send the signed hard copy along with self-attested copies of all supporting documents (educational certificates, experience certificates, category certificate, fee receipt, credit point summary, etc.) by Speed Post / Registered Post to:
   > The Dean (Faculty Welfare),
   > National Institute of Technology Silchar,
   > P.O. REC Silchar – 788010,
   > Dist. Cachar, Assam

   Envelope must be superscribed with “Application for the Post of …………… in the Department of ……………”.

Government employees must apply through proper channel or submit NOC at the time of interview.

### Important Links
- **Apply Online**: [https://recruitment.nits.ac.in/](https://recruitment.nits.ac.in/)
- **Official Notification**: Check NIT Silchar website for detailed PDF.

### FAQs
**Q1: What is the total number of vacancies in NIT Silchar Faculty Recruitment 2026?**
Professor: 35 posts (9 Fresh + 26 Backlog). Applications are also invited for Associate Professor and Assistant Professor posts in various departments.

**Q2: What are the pay scales for different faculty posts?**
Professor: Level-14A | Associate Professor: Level-13A2 | Assistant Professor Grade-I: Level-12 | Assistant Professor Grade-II: Level-10

**Q3: What is the application fee?**
₹1,200 for General/OBC/EWS and ₹600 for SC/ST/PwD candidates.

**Q4: What is the selection process?**
Shortlisting followed by Interview.

**Q5: What is the last date to apply?**
Online application: 04 July 2026. Hard copy must reach within 10 days after online closing.

**Q6: Which departments are inviting applications?**
Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, ECE, Computer Science & Engineering, Mathematics, Humanities & Social Sciences, and Management Studies.

content:
## SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026 – Apply Online for 10 Posts

Satish Dhawan Space Centre SHAR (ISRO) invites online applications from eligible candidates for recruitment of Trained Graduate Teachers (TGT) and Primary Teachers (PRT) for its schools at Sriharikota and Sullurpeta. The SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026 (ISRO) for 10 Vacancies Online Application process starts from 16th June 2026 and closes on 07th July 2026.

### Vacancy Details
| Post Name | No. of Vacancies |
|-----------|------------------|
| TGT (Maths) | 01 (UR) |
| TGT (Science) | 01 (SC) |
| TGT (Work Experience Teacher) | 01 (UR) |
| TGT (Telugu) | 01 (ST) |
| Primary Teacher (PRT) | 06 (UR-3, EWS-1, OBC-2) |
| **Total** | **10** |

### Eligibility Criteria
| Post | Age Limit (as on 07.07.2026) | Essential Qualification |
|------|------------------------------|------------------------|
| TGT (Maths) | 18 – 35 years | Degree with Mathematics as main subject + B.Ed (NCTE recognized) + CTET Paper-II + Ability to teach in Hindi & English |
| TGT (Science) | 18 – 35 years | Degree with Chemistry + Biology/Botany/Zoology + B.Ed + CTET Paper-II + Ability to teach in Hindi & English |
| TGT (Work Experience Teacher) | 18 – 35 years | Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical / Electronics / Electronics & Communication Engineering (min 50% marks) + B.Ed + Knowledge of Computers |
| TGT (Telugu) | 18 – 35 years | Bachelor’s / PG Degree in Telugu + B.Ed with Telugu methodology or Telugu Pandit Training + CTET/State TET Paper-II |
| Primary Teacher (PRT) | 18 – 30 years | 10+2 with 50% marks + D.El.Ed / B.El.Ed / Special Education + CTET Paper-I + Ability to teach in Hindi & English |

### Application Fee
| Category | Fee | Refund after appearing in Written Test |
|----------|-----|----------------------------------------|
| All candidates | ₹750 (₹250 Application + ₹500 Processing) | Women / SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen: Full ₹750 refund; Others: ₹500 refund (₹250 retained) |

### Important Dates
| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Notification Date | 16 June 2026 |
| Online Application Start Date | 16 June 2026 |
| Last Date for Online Application | 07 July 2026 |
| Age Limit as on | 07 July 2026 |

### Selection Process
The selection will be done through the following stages:
1. Written Test (Computer Based Test - CBT)
2. Skill Test (Teaching Demonstration) – Qualifying in nature (Minimum 60% marks out of 100)
Final merit list will be prepared on the basis of marks obtained in the Written Test among the candidates who qualify the Skill Test.

### How to Apply
Candidates must apply online only through the official SDSC SHAR / ISRO recruitment portal. The steps are as follows:
1. Visit the official apply online link and register yourself.
2. Fill the online application form carefully with all required details.
3. Upload scanned copies of Passport size photograph, Signature, Educational certificates, Category/PwBD certificates (if applicable), and NOC (if employed in Govt. organization).
4. Pay the application fee of ₹750 online.
5. Submit the application form and take a printout for future reference.

### Important Links
- [Detailed Notification PDF](#)
- [Apply Online Link](#)
- Join Ind Govt Jobs Alert Channel: Telegram | WhatsApp | Email Alert

### FAQs
**Q: What is the total number of vacancies in SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026?**
A: 10 posts (TGT – 4 posts & PRT – 6 posts).

**Q: What is the age limit for SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026?**
A: TGT Posts: 18 – 35 years | Primary Teacher (PRT): 18 – 30 years (as on 07.07.2026) with applicable relaxations.

**Q: What is the pay scale in SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026?**
A: TGT: Level-7 (₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400) | Primary Teacher: Level-6 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400)

**Q: What is the selection process for SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026?**
A: Written Test (CBT) + Skill Test (Teaching Demonstration – Qualifying with minimum 60% marks). Final merit based on Written Test marks.

**Q: What is the application fee for SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026?**
A: ₹750 per application. Full or partial refund available after appearing in Written Test as per category.

**Q: What is the last date to apply for SDSC SHAR Teacher Recruitment 2026?**
A: 07 July 2026.

content:
## Employment News 20 – 26 June 2026: Weekly Govt Job Notifications

### Overview
Employment News is the official weekly newspaper of the Government of India, publishing job vacancies from Central and State Governments, PSUs, Railways, Banks, and more. This edition covers 20th to 26th June 2026.

### Application Fee
| Category | Fee |
|----------|-----|
| General / OBC | ₹100 - ₹500 (varies by post) |
| SC / ST / PwD | Nil (or exempted) |
| Female | Nil (or exempted) |

### Important Dates
| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Issue Date | 20 June 2026 |
| Last Date for Applications | Varies (see table below) |

### Vacancy Highlights
| Post Name | Total Vacancies | Last Date | Organization |
|-----------|----------------|-----------|--------------|
| ITI & Non ITI Apprentice | 495 | 01/07/2026 | Mazagon Dock (MDL) |
| Junior Engineers | 175 | 14/07/2026 | NHSRCL |
| Deputy Engineer and Other Posts | 34 | 14/07/2026 | NHSRCL |
| Non Teaching Posts | 24 | 01/07/2026 | Lakshmibai College |
| Manager Level Posts | 10 | 06/07/2026 | Mumbai Port Authority |
| Executive Posts | 04 | 26/06/2026 | NTPC Limited |
| Various Ministries Posts | 538 | 03/07/2026 | UPSC Advt No. 06/2026 |
| Group A, B & C Posts | 179 | 31/07/2026 | CCRUM |
| Senior Field Officer (Technical) | 25 | 12/07/2026 | Cabinet Secretariat |
| Group B & Group C Posts | 1979 | 15/07/2026 | DSSSB |
| Group A Non Faculty Posts | 34 | 01/07/2026 | JIPMER |
| Engineer, Geologist, Economist, Chemist etc. | 10 | 28/06/2026 | Oil India Limited |
| Manager Level Posts | 10 | 06/07/2026 | Mumbai Port Authority |
| Manager-Civil / Engineer-Civil | 02 | 21/06/2026 | RITES Limited |

### How to Apply
1. Visit the official Employment News website: [www.employmentnews.gov.in](https://www.employmentnews.gov.in).
2. Download the PDF of the 20-26 June 2026 issue.
3. Browse through the notifications and select the desired post.
4. Click on the apply link provided in the notification (redirects to the respective organization's portal).
5. Complete the online application form, upload documents, and pay the fee (if applicable).
6. Submit and save a copy for reference.

### FAQs
**Q: What is Employment News?**
A: It is a weekly newspaper that publishes latest govt job vacancies, exam notices, and results.

**Q: How to get Employment News online?**
A: Visit [www.employmentnews.gov.in](https://www.employmentnews.gov.in) and subscribe to the e-version.

**Q: What is the subscription cost?**
A: ₹400 for 1 year, ₹750 for 2 years, ₹1050 for 3 years.

**Q: What types of jobs are listed?**
A: Central/State Govt, PSU, Railway, Banking, Defence, and more.

### Important Links
- Official Website: [https://www.employmentnews.gov.in](https://www.employmentnews.gov.in)
- Subscribe to e-version: [https://e-employmentnews.co.in](https://e-employmentnews.co.in)

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Application Start | Varies by post |
| Last Date to Apply | As per table above |
| Exam Date | To be announced |
| Admit Card Release | 1-2 weeks before exam |

### Eligibility Criteria

- **Educational Qualification:** Minimum 10th/SSLC/Matriculation, 12th, Diploma, ITI, Any Degree, or Post Graduate as per post requirements.
- **Age Limit:** Minimum 18 years. Upper age relaxation for reserved categories as per government norms.
- **Language Requirement:** Other state candidates must have studied Tamil as a subject in Matriculation/10th class.

### How to Apply

1. Visit the official website of the respective recruitment body (e.g., tnpsc.gov.in, tn.gov.in).
2. Find the notification for the desired post.
3. Read the notification thoroughly and check eligibility.
4. Click on 'Apply Online' or download the application form.
5. Fill in all required details, upload documents, and pay the fee if applicable.
6. Submit the application and take a printout for future reference.

### Selection Process

Selection may involve a written exam, skill test, interview, or document verification depending on the post. For TNPSC posts, exams are conducted for Group I, II, IV, VAO, and Engineer posts.

### Official Websites

- Tamil Nadu Government Employment Portal: [tn.gov.in](https://www.tn.gov.in)
- Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission: [tnpsc.gov.in](https://www.tnpsc.gov.in)
- TN Velai Vaippu: [tnvelaivaaippu.gov.in](https://www.tnvelaivaaippu.gov.in)
- Medical Services Recruitment Board: [mrb.tn.gov.in](https://www.mrb.tn.gov.in)

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**Q: What type of jobs are available in Tamil Nadu Government?**
A: TN Govt offers Group IV, Group II, VAO, Engineer, Apprentice, Assistant, Typist, Stenographer, Clerk, Driver, Manager, and other departmental posts.

**Q: How to apply for Tamil Nadu Government Jobs?**
A: Eligible candidates should check the notification, visit the official website, and apply online or submit the prescribed application form.

**Q: What are the qualifications for TN Govt Jobs?**
A: Minimum 10th/SSLC/Matriculation, 12th, Diploma, ITI, Any Degree, or Post Graduate.

**Q: What is the age limit?**
A: Minimum 18 years. Maximum age varies; reserved categories have no upper age limit.

**Q: Can other state candidates apply?**
A: Yes, but they must have knowledge of Tamil (studied Tamil as a subject in Matriculation/10th class).

**Q: Is this page updated regularly?**
A: Yes, IndGovtJobs updates the TN Govt Jobs page every week with the latest notifications.

## UP Police Constable Answer Key 2026

The Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board has released the provisional answer key for the Constable (General Selection) recruitment examination 2026. Candidates can download the answer key and their response sheet from the official website.

### Important Dates

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Answer Key Release | To be announced |
| Last Date to Raise Objections | To be announced |
| Final Result | To be announced |

### How to Download Answer Key

1. Visit the official website: [uppbpb.gov.in](https://uppbpb.gov.in)
2. Click on the 'Answer Key' link under the 'Recruitment' section.
3. Enter your registration number and password/date of birth.
4. The answer key and your response sheet will be displayed.
5. Download and save for future reference.

### Objection Process

If you find any discrepancy in the answer key, you can raise an objection online by paying a nominal fee per question. Ensure you provide valid supporting documents.

### Exam Day Guidelines

- Carry a printed copy of the admit card and a valid photo ID.
- Reach the exam center at least 30 minutes before the reporting time.
- Follow all COVID-19 protocols if applicable.

### Verification Documents

Shortlisted candidates will be required to produce original documents for verification, including:
- Educational qualification certificates
- Age proof
- Caste certificate (if applicable)
- Domicile certificate
- Character certificate

Stay tuned for further updates on the final result and subsequent selection stages.

title: SBI PO Recruitment 2026
overview: The State Bank of India (SBI) is set to release its highly anticipated notification for the recruitment of Probationary Officers (PO) for the year 2026. This significant announcement opens up a gateway for approximately 1500 aspiring candidates seeking a prestigious career in India's largest public sector bank. The SBI PO examination is renowned for its rigorous selection process, offering a dynamic work environment, attractive remuneration, and promising career advancement opportunities. Candidates are strongly advised to regularly monitor the official SBI careers portal for the detailed notification, exact vacancy breakdown, and crucial application timelines.
eligibility: Candidates must hold a Graduation degree in any discipline from a recognized University or possess an equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government. Those in their final year/semester of graduation may also apply provisionally, provided they produce proof of having passed the graduation examination on or before a specified date (to be announced in the official notification).
age_limit: Candidates must be between 21 and 30 years of age as of a specific cut-off date (to be mentioned in the official notification). Age relaxation will be applicable for candidates belonging to reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen) as per Government of India rules.
salary: The starting basic pay for SBI POs is currently ₹41,800/- (with 4 advance increments). The total compensation package includes Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), City Compensatory Allowance (CCA), Medical Allowance, and other benefits, making the total emoluments attractive. The annual CTC (Cost to Company) typically ranges from ₹8.20 Lakhs to ₹13.08 Lakhs depending on the place of posting.
how_to_apply:
Applying for the SBI PO Recruitment 2026 will typically involve an online process. Candidates are advised to follow these general steps once the official notification and application portal are active:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Navigate to the 'Careers' section on the official State Bank of India website (bank.sbi/careers or sbi.co.in/careers).
2.  **Find the Notification:** Look for the 'Recruitment of Probationary Officers (PO) 2026' link and carefully read the official notification PDF.
3.  **Registration:** Click on 'New Registration' and provide essential details like name, contact number, and email ID to generate a provisional registration number and password.
4.  **Fill Application Form:** Log in with the generated credentials and accurately fill out the online application form with personal, educational, and communication details.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph and signature in the prescribed format and size.
6.  **Pay Application Fee:** Pay the requisite application fee online using debit card/credit card/net banking. Ensure to save the e-receipt.
7.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all entered details before final submission. No changes will be allowed after submission.
8.  **Print Application:** Take a printout of the completed application form for your records.
meta_title: SBI PO Recruitment 2026 2026: Online Form, Eligibility & Benefits
meta_description: The State Bank of India (SBI) is set to release its highly anticipated notification for the recruitment of Probationary Officers (PO) for the year 2026. Th
content:
## SBI PO Recruitment 2026: Your Gateway to a Stellar Banking Career

The State Bank of India (SBI), a leader in the Indian banking sector, is preparing to announce its Probationary Officer (PO) Recruitment for the year 2026. This is a highly sought-after opportunity for graduates aiming for a challenging and rewarding career. SBI POs are responsible for various banking operations, customer service, and credit management, playing a crucial role in the bank's success.

### Vacancy Details

While the detailed official notification is awaited, the recruitment is expected to announce approximately **1500 Probationary Officer (PO)** vacancies across India. The exact breakdown of vacancies category-wise will be provided in the comprehensive notification.

### Important Dates

Candidates should regularly check the official SBI careers portal for the precise schedule. The dates for the 2026 cycle are anticipated to be announced in late 2025.

| Event                           | Date / Status                      |
| :------------------------------ | :--------------------------------- |
| Release of Official Notification| To be announced (Expected Nov-Dec 2025) |
| Online Application Start Date   | To be announced                    |
| Online Application End Date     | To be announced                    |
| Call Letter for Prelims Exam    | To be announced                    |
| Preliminary Examination         | To be announced (Expected Jan 2026) |
| Result of Prelims Exam          | To be announced                    |
| Call Letter for Mains Exam      | To be announced                    |
| Main Examination                | To be announced (Expected Feb-Mar 2026) |
| Result of Mains Exam            | To be announced                    |
| Interview/Group Exercise        | To be announced                    |
| Final Result Declaration        | To be announced                    |

### Application Fee

Application fees are typically non-refundable and vary by category. Based on previous recruitments, the expected fee structure is:

| Category           | Application Fee (Expected) |
| :----------------- | :------------------------- |
| General/EWS/OBC    | ₹750/-                     |
| SC/ST/PwBD         | ₹0/-                       |

*Payment will be accepted online via Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking.*

### Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the SBI PO 2026 recruitment, candidates must meet the following criteria:

*   **Educational Qualification:** A candidate must possess a Graduation degree in any discipline from a recognized University or hold an equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government. Those in the final year/semester of their graduation may also apply provisionally, provided they submit proof of having passed the graduation examination on or before the date stipulated in the official notification.
*   **Age Limit:** Candidates should be between 21 years and 30 years of age as of a specific cut-off date (to be announced). Age relaxation will be provided to reserved categories as per government norms:
    *   SC/ST: 5 years
    *   OBC (Non-Creamy Layer): 3 years
    *   PwBD (General/EWS): 10 years
    *   PwBD (SC/ST): 15 years
    *   PwBD (OBC): 13 years
    *   Ex-Servicemen: As per government rules

### Selection Process

The SBI PO selection process is rigorous and comprises three phases:

1.  **Phase I: Preliminary Examination:** An online objective test consisting of English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, and Reasoning Ability. Marks obtained in the Preliminary Examination will not be added for the final merit list.
2.  **Phase II: Main Examination:** An online objective test (Reasoning & Computer Aptitude, Data Analysis & Interpretation, General Economy/Banking/Awareness, English Language) and an objective test (English Language - Letter Writing & Essay).
3.  **Phase III: Interview & Group Exercise:** Candidates qualifying the Main Examination will be called for an Interview and Group Exercise.

### Salary and Career Path

Selected Probationary Officers receive an attractive compensation package and robust career growth opportunities. The current starting basic pay is ₹41,800/- (with 4 advance increments). Along with this, they receive Dearness Allowance, HRA, CCA, Medical, and other allowances. The total annual CTC ranges from ₹8.20 Lakhs to ₹13.08 Lakhs. SBI offers excellent career progression through promotions, allowing POs to reach senior management positions within the bank and its various subsidiaries.

### How to Apply for SBI PO Recruitment 2026

The application process for SBI PO is entirely online. Follow these steps once the application window opens:

1.  **Access the Careers Page:** Visit the official SBI careers website at `https://bank.sbi/careers` or `https://sbi.co.in/careers`.
2.  **Locate the Recruitment Link:** Click on the 'Latest Announcements' or 'Recruitment of Probationary Officers' link for the 2026 cycle.
3.  **Read the Notification:** Download and thoroughly read the official notification PDF to understand all eligibility criteria, important dates, and instructions.
4.  **New Registration:** Click on the 'APPLY ONLINE' link and then 'Click Here for New Registration'.
5.  **Fill Basic Information:** Enter your Name, Contact details, and Email ID. A provisional Registration Number and Password will be generated and sent to your registered email ID and mobile number.
6.  **Complete Application Form:** Log in using the generated credentials and fill in all required details in the application form, including personal, educational, and communication information. Ensure accuracy as no changes can be made after final submission.
7.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned images of your photograph and signature as per the specifications mentioned in the notification (dimensions, file size, format).
8.  **Preview Application:** Review all the entered information carefully. Make any necessary edits before proceeding.
9.  **Pay Application Fee:** Select your category and proceed to pay the application fee online using Debit Card, Credit Card, or Internet Banking. An e-receipt will be generated upon successful payment.
10. **Final Submission & Print:** After successful payment, click on 'SUBMIT'. Take a printout of the e-receipt and the submitted application form for future reference.

### Important Links

*   **Official SBI Careers Website:** [https://bank.sbi/careers](https://bank.sbi/careers)
*   **Official Notification (Link will be active when released):** To be announced
*   **Apply Online (Link will be active when released):** To be announced

## NEIGRIHMS Faculty Recruitment 2026 – 52 Vacancies

North Eastern Indira Gandhi Regional Institute of Health & Medical Sciences (NEIGRIHMS), Shillong invites applications for Group ‘A’ Faculty posts on Direct Recruitment/Deputation basis.

### Vacancy Details
| Post | Total Vacancies |
|------|----------------|
| Professor | As per notification |
| Associate Professor | As per notification |
| Assistant Professor | As per notification |

Specialties include Anaesthesiology, Biochemistry, Cardiology, CTVS, General Medicine, General Surgery, Medical Oncology, Microbiology, Nephrology, Neurosurgery, Neurology, Nuclear Medicine, Obstetrics & Gynaecology, Ophthalmology, Paediatrics, Pathology, Pharmacology, Radiodiagnosis, Radiation Oncology, Surgical Oncology, TB & Respiratory Disease, Transfusion Medicine, Urology, and others.

### Application Fee
| Category | Fee |
|----------|-----|
| General/OBC/EWS | ₹500 |
| SC/ST | ₹250 |
| PwBD | Nil |

|-------|------|
| Notification Release | 20 June 2026 |

- **Professor**: MBBS + PG Degree + 14 years teaching/research experience (broad specialty) or as per super-specialty norms. Age: 58 years.

content:
## Bank of Baroda Recruitment 2026: An Overview

Bank of Baroda (BoB), one of India's leading public sector banks, is set to open doors for aspiring banking professionals with an announcement for 86 vacant positions in 2026. This recruitment drive offers a significant opportunity for individuals seeking to establish a career in the dynamic banking sector. While the detailed official notification for the 2026 recruitment is yet to be released, candidates are encouraged to prepare and stay updated with the Bank's official communications.

### Important Dates & Schedule (Tentative)

As the official notification for 2026 is awaited, the following dates are tentative and based on typical recruitment cycles. Candidates must refer to the official notification upon its release for precise timelines.

| Event                                 | Tentative Date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| :------------------------------------ | :-------------------------- |
| Notification Release                  | To be announced (Early 2026) |
| Online Application Start Date         | To be announced             |
| Online Application End Date           | To be announced             |
| Last Date for Fee Payment             | To be announced             |
| Admit Card Release for Online Exam    | To be announced             |
| Online Examination Date               | To be announced             |
| Result Declaration                    | To be announced             |

### Application Fee

The application fee structure for Bank of Baroda recruitment is generally as follows. Exact fees will be confirmed in the official 2026 notification.

| Category               | Application Fee (INR) |
| :--------------------- | :-------------------- |
| General/EWS/OBC        | To be announced       |
| SC/ST/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen | To be announced       |

*Note: Fees are non-refundable and must be paid through online modes (Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, etc.).*

### Post Qualifications

The 86 posts announced under Bank of Baroda Recruitment 2026 are expected to cover a range of positions, including but not limited to Probationary Officers (PO), Clerks, and various Specialist Officers. The minimum educational qualifications are generally as follows:

*   **For Clerical/Officer Cadre:** A Bachelor's degree in any discipline from a University recognized by the Govt. of India or any equivalent qualification recognized as such by the Central Government. Candidates must possess a valid mark-sheet / degree certificate that they are a graduate on the day they register and indicate the percentage of marks obtained in Graduation while registering online.
*   **For Specialist Officer Cadre:** Specific professional qualifications (e.g., Engineering degree, MBA, CA, Law degree) relevant to the specialization, along with post-qualification experience, will be required. These will be detailed for each specific post.

### Age Limit (As on a specific cut-off date to be announced)

The age criteria for various posts are typically:

*   **Minimum Age:** 20 years
*   **Maximum Age:** 28 - 30 years (depending on the post)

**Age Relaxation:**
Age relaxation will be provided to candidates belonging to reserved categories as per Government of India rules:

*   SC/ST: 5 years
*   OBC (Non-Creamy Layer): 3 years
*   Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD): 10 years
*   Ex-Servicemen: As per government norms.

The exact cut-off date for age calculation will be specified in the official notification.

### Vacancy Details

Bank of Baroda has announced a total of **86 vacancies** for the 2026 recruitment drive. The detailed post-wise and category-wise distribution of these vacancies will be provided in the official notification when released.

### How to Apply

Candidates interested in applying for the Bank of Baroda Recruitment 2026 should follow these steps once the official notification and application portal are live:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official Bank of Baroda website (www.bankofbaroda.in) and navigate to the 'Career' or 'Recruitment' section.
2.  **Find the Notification:** Locate the 'Bank of Baroda Recruitment 2026' notification link and download the detailed PDF to read all instructions, eligibility criteria, and important dates carefully.
3.  **New Registration:** Click on 'New Registration' or 'Apply Online' link. Provide basic details like name, contact number, email ID, etc., to generate a provisional registration number and password.
4.  **Fill Application Form:** Log in using the registration credentials and fill out the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and professional details.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration as per the specifications mentioned in the notification.
6.  **Pay Application Fee:** Proceed to pay the application fee online using Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or other available options. Ensure successful transaction.
7.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the entered information before final submission. No changes will be allowed after final submission.
8.  **Print Application:** After successful submission and payment, take a printout of the e-receipt and the submitted application form for future reference.

content:
# NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card Released by NTA

The National Testing Agency (NTA) has activated the download link for the NEET UG 2026 Re-Examination Admit Cards. This is a critical update for all candidates preparing to appear for the re-exam. The admit card serves as your official pass to the examination center and contains all necessary details regarding your test. It is crucial to download, verify, and print your admit card well before the examination date.

## Important Schedules/Timelines

Candidates should keep track of the following key dates related to the NEET UG 2026 Re-Examination:

| Event                                 | Date / Status            |
| :------------------------------------ | :----------------------- |
| NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card Release | **Available Now**        |
| NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Date             | *[Date to be updated]*   |
| Last Date to Download Admit Card      | *[Date to be updated]*   |
| Provisional Answer Key Release        | *[Date to be updated]*   |
| Final Answer Key & Result Declaration | *[Date to be updated]*   |


## How to Download Your NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card

Follow these simple steps to successfully download your admit card from the official NTA NEET website:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the National Testing Agency's official NEET portal at `https://neet.nta.nic.in/`.
2.  **Locate Admit Card Link:** On the homepage, look for a prominent link that says "NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card Download" or similar.
3.  **Login Credentials:** Click on the link. You will be redirected to a login page where you need to enter your:
    *   Application Number
    *   Date of Birth
    *   Security Pin (as displayed on the screen)
4.  **Submit Details:** After entering the required information, click on the "Submit" or "Login" button.
5.  **View and Download:** Your NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card will be displayed on the screen. Verify all the details carefully.
6.  **Print Admit Card:** Download the PDF version of your admit card and take at least two clear printouts. It is advised to keep a digital copy for future reference as well.

## Exam Day Guidelines and Mandatory Documents

To ensure a smooth examination experience, candidates must adhere to the following guidelines and carry the specified documents:

*   **Admit Card:** A printed copy of your NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card with your photograph and signature clearly visible.
*   **Valid Photo ID Proof:** Bring one original, valid government-issued photo identification proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, Passport, PAN Card, Driving License, Voter ID). No photocopies will be accepted.
*   **Passport Size Photograph:** Carry one additional recent passport-size photograph (same as uploaded on the application form) to be pasted on the attendance sheet at the examination centre.
*   **PwBD Certificate (if applicable):** If you are a PwBD candidate, carry your original PwBD certificate.
*   **Reporting Time:** Reach the examination centre at the reporting time mentioned on your admit card to complete frisking and other formalities.
*   **Prohibited Items:** Do not carry any electronic gadgets, mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, study material, or any other unauthorized items into the examination hall. Follow all instructions regarding items allowed/not allowed.
*   **Self-Declaration:** Candidates may be required to fill a self-declaration form regarding their health status as per NTA guidelines.

## Important Links

| Resource                               | Link                       |
| :------------------------------------- | :------------------------- |
| **Direct Link to Download Admit Card** | https://neet.nta.nic.in/   |
| Official NTA NEET Website              | https://neet.nta.nic.in/   |

content:
## NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card Released

The National Testing Agency (NTA) has made available the Admit Cards for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET UG) 2026 Re-Examination. All candidates who have registered for this crucial medical entrance test are advised to download their admit cards immediately to avoid last-minute rush and technical issues. The admit card is an essential document, without which entry to the examination centre will be denied. It contains important details such as your roll number, examination centre address, exam date, and reporting time.

### Important Dates for NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam

| Event                                      | Date (Tentative) |
| :----------------------------------------- | :--------------- |
| NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card Release    | To Be Updated    |
| NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Date                  | 2026-06-23       |
| NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Result Announcement   | To Be Updated    |

*Note: Candidates are advised to regularly check the official NTA NEET website for the most accurate and updated schedule.*

### How to Download Your NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card

Follow these simple steps to download your admit card:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official NTA NEET website: `neet.nta.nic.in`.
2.  **Locate Admit Card Link:** On the homepage, find and click on the link titled 'NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card Download'.
3.  **Login Credentials:** You will be redirected to a login page. Enter your **Application Number** and **Date of Birth** (in DD/MM/YYYY format).
4.  **Security Pin:** Input the security pin (captcha code) displayed on the screen.
5.  **Submit:** Click on the 'Submit' or 'Login' button.
6.  **View and Verify:** Your Admit Card will appear on the screen. Carefully check all the details, including your name, photograph, signature, exam centre, date, and time.
7.  **Download and Print:** Download the PDF file of your admit card and take at least two clear printouts. Ensure all details are clearly visible.

### Exam Day Guidelines and Instructions

Candidates must strictly adhere to the following guidelines on the day of the examination:

*   **Reporting Time:** Reach the examination centre well before the gate closing time as mentioned on your admit card. Latecomers will not be allowed to enter.
*   **Admit Card:** Carry a printed copy of your NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam Admit Card with a clear photograph pasted on it (if not already printed).
*   **Photo ID Proof:** Bring one original, valid photo identification proof (e.g., Aadhar Card, Passport, Driving License, PAN Card, Voter ID). Photocopies or scanned copies will not be accepted.
*   **Photograph:** Carry an additional passport-size photograph (same as uploaded in the application form) to be affixed on the attendance sheet.
*   **Prohibited Items:** Do NOT carry any electronic gadgets (mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators), study material, bags, or any other unauthorized items into the examination hall. Only transparent ballpoint pens are usually allowed.
*   **Dress Code:** Follow the prescribed dress code to avoid any inconvenience during security checks.
*   **Rough Work:** All rough work must be done only in the space provided in the test booklet.
*   **COVID-19 Precautions:** Follow all prevailing COVID-19 safety protocols, including wearing a mask, maintaining social distancing, and carrying a personal hand sanitizer.

### Documents Required at the Examination Centre

content:
## DSSSB Exam 2026 Admit Card Released

**Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB)** has officially released the Admit Cards for the various examinations scheduled for 2026. Candidates who have successfully applied for the respective posts are advised to download their e-admit cards immediately to prepare for the upcoming examination.

The admit card is a mandatory document for entry into the examination hall. It contains crucial details such as the candidate's name, roll number, photograph, signature, examination date, time, venue, and important instructions.

### Important Dates/Schedules

| Event                          | Date                  |
| :----------------------------- | :-------------------- |
| Application Start Date         | As per Notification   |
| Application End Date           | As per Notification   |
| Admit Card Release Date        | Announced / Released  |
| DSSSB Exam 2026 Date           | To be announced shortly |
| Result Declaration             | To be announced       |

### Admit Card Download Instructions

To download your DSSSB Exam 2026 Admit Card, please follow these steps:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Navigate to the official website of the Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) at `dsssb.delhi.gov.in`.
2.  **Locate Admit Card Link:** On the homepage, find and click on the link titled 'Download Admit Card for DSSSB Exam 2026' or similar under the 'Latest News' or 'Notifications' section.
3.  **Enter Credentials:** You will be redirected to a login page. Enter your Application Number/Registration ID and your Date of Birth (DD/MM/YYYY) or Password as required.
4.  **Submit and View:** After entering the details, click on the 'Submit' or 'Login' button.
5.  **Download Admit Card:** Your DSSSB Exam 2026 Admit Card will be displayed on the screen. Verify all the details carefully.
6.  **Print Out:** Download the admit card and take at least two clear printouts for future reference and for carrying to the examination center.

### Exam Day Guidelines and Important Instructions

Candidates must adhere to the following guidelines on the day of the examination:

*   **Mandatory Documents:** Candidates must carry a printed copy of their DSSSB Admit Card and a valid original photo identity proof (e.g., Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Driving License, Passport) to the examination center. Without these, entry will be denied.
*   **Reporting Time:** Reach the examination venue well before the reporting time mentioned on your admit card to complete necessary formalities.
*   **Prohibited Items:** Do not carry any electronic gadgets such as mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, or any other unauthorized material into the examination hall.
*   **Rough Work:** All rough work must be done only on the rough sheets provided at the examination center. Do not write anything on the admit card or question paper.
*   **COVID-19 Protocols (if applicable for 2026):** Follow all health and safety guidelines, including maintaining social distancing, wearing a mask, and carrying a personal hand sanitizer, if mandated by the authorities.
*   **Signature and Photograph:** Ensure the photograph and signature on the admit card are clear and match your appearance.

Candidates are strongly advised to thoroughly read all instructions mentioned on their admit card and on the official DSSSB website for any further updates or changes regarding the examination.

content:
## UPSC Civil Service 2026 Admit Card Details

The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has made the admit cards available for the upcoming Civil Service Examination (CSE) 2026. This admit card is mandatory for all candidates appearing for the examination. It serves as proof of your candidature and contains critical information about your exam schedule and venue. Please ensure you download and review your admit card thoroughly.

### Important Dates for UPSC CSE 2026 Admit Card

Refer to the table below for key dates related to the UPSC Civil Service 2026 Admit Card download and examination schedule.

| Event                      | Date                  |
| :------------------------- | :-------------------- |
| Admit Card Release         | Available Now         |
| UPSC CSE 2026 Exam (Prelims) | To be announced (2026)|
| UPSC CSE 2026 Exam (Mains)   | To be announced (2026)|
| Result Announcement        | To be announced       |

Candidates are advised to regularly check the official UPSC website for the latest updates on exam dates and schedules.

### Exam Day Guidelines and Instructions

To ensure a smooth examination experience, candidates must adhere to the following guidelines:

*   **Admit Card:** Carry a printed copy of your e-Admit Card to the examination venue. No candidate will be allowed to appear for the examination without a valid admit card.
*   **Photo ID Proof:** Bring one original, valid photo identity proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Passport, Driving License) along with a photocopy.
*   **Reporting Time:** Reach the examination centre well in advance as per the reporting time mentioned on your admit card to complete necessary formalities.
*   **Prohibited Items:** Do not carry any electronic gadgets such as mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, Bluetooth devices, or any other unauthorized material into the examination hall.
*   **Rough Work:** All rough work must be done only on the sheets provided in the examination hall.
*   **COVID-19 Protocols:** Candidates are advised to follow all prevailing government guidelines regarding COVID-19, including wearing masks, maintaining social distancing, and using hand sanitizers.

### Documents Required on Exam Day

Candidates must carry the following documents to the examination centre:

1.  **Printed e-Admit Card:** A clear printout of the downloaded admit card.
2.  **Original Photo Identity Proof:** One valid photo ID (e.g., Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Passport, Driving License).
3.  **Passport-size Photographs:** Two recent passport-size photographs, preferably similar to the one uploaded during the application process, in case they are required for attendance sheet verification.

### Important Instructions for Candidates

*   **Verify Details:** Carefully check all the particulars printed on your admit card. In case of any discrepancy (e.g., name, photograph, roll number, exam centre), immediately contact the UPSC examination authorities.
*   **Read Instructions:** Go through all the instructions mentioned on the admit card and in the official information bulletin thoroughly.
*   **Preserve Admit Card:** Keep your admit card safe even after the examination, as it may be required during subsequent stages of the selection process.
*   **No Entry After Gate Closing:** Candidates will not be permitted to enter the examination venue after the specified gate closing time.

content:
### Uttar Pradesh Board Class 10th & 12th Result 2026 Update

The Uttar Pradesh Madhyamik Shiksha Parishad (UPMSP) is preparing to release the much-awaited results for the Class 10th (High School) and Class 12th (Intermediate) Board Examinations for the academic session 2025-26. Students who diligently appeared for these examinations across the state are eagerly anticipating their scores, which will be instrumental in shaping their academic and career trajectories.

#### Key Information for Students

Upon declaration, students will be able to access their provisional mark sheets online. It is crucial to have your roll number readily available to quickly check your result. The online result will provide a detailed breakdown of marks obtained in each subject, along with the overall qualifying status.

#### Important Dates/Timelines

| Event                           | Date            |
| :------------------------------ | :-------------- |
| Class 10th Examination Period   | To be announced |
| Class 12th Examination Period   | To be announced |
| **Result Declaration Date**     | **To be announced** |
| Application for Re-evaluation/Scrutiny | To be announced |
| Original Marksheet Distribution | To be announced |

*Note: All dates are tentative and will be updated immediately upon official announcement by UPMSP.*

#### How to Check Your UP Board Result 2026

Follow these straightforward steps to check and download your Uttar Pradesh Board Class 10th or 12th result:

1.  **Access the Official Portal:** Go to the designated official results website of UPMSP, which is typically `https://upresults.nic.in/` or `https://upmsp.edu.in/results`.
2.  **Locate Result Link:** On the homepage, find and click on the link specifically for 'UP Board High School (Class 10th) Examination Result 2026' or 'UP Board Intermediate (Class 12th) Examination Result 2026', as per your class.
3.  **Input Credentials:** A new window or page will appear. Enter your unique 7-digit Roll Number in the specified field. You might also be required to enter your School Code or complete a security captcha.
4.  **View Your Result:** Click on the 'Submit' or 'View Result' button. Your comprehensive scorecard will be displayed on your screen.
5.  **Download and Print:** Carefully verify all the details on your provisional mark sheet. It is highly advisable to download the e-result and take a printout for your personal records, future academic admissions, and reference until the original certificates are issued.

#### Post-Result Information

*   **Original Documents:** The online mark sheet is provisional. Original mark sheets, pass certificates, and migration certificates will be distributed by your respective schools a few weeks after the online declaration.
*   **Re-evaluation/Scrutiny Process:** Students who believe there has been an error in the marking of their answer sheets can apply for re-evaluation or scrutiny. The application window and detailed instructions for this process will be released by UPMSP post-result declaration.
*   **Future Education:** Based on the results, Class 10th pass-outs can enroll in Class 11th (Intermediate) or vocational courses, while Class 12th pass-outs can apply for various undergraduate degree programs, diplomas, or professional courses.

Stay updated by regularly visiting the official UPMSP website for all the latest notifications regarding results, supplementary exams, and document distribution.

| Technical Officer, Technician Posts | – | 20/06/2026 | CEERI Pilani |

### Application Fee

| Category | Fee (₹) |
|----------|---------|
| General / OBC | Varies by post (typically ₹100-₹500) |
| SC / ST / PWD | Nil or reduced fee |
| Women | Nil (in some cases) |

*Note: Exact fee details are mentioned in the official notification of each post.*

### Important Dates (General Timeline)

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Application Start | Varies by post |
| Last Date to Apply | As per table above |
| Exam Date (Tentative) | To be announced |
| Admit Card Release | 1-2 weeks before exam |
| Result Declaration | 2-3 months after exam |

### Qualifications & Age Limit

- **Educational Qualification:** Minimum 10th pass; for most posts, 12th pass, Diploma, Degree, or PG required.
- **Age Limit:** Minimum 18 years. Upper age relaxation as per government norms for reserved categories.

### How to Apply

1. Visit the official website of the recruiting department (e.g., tnpsc.gov.in, tn.gov.in).
2. Find the notification for the desired post.
3. Read the eligibility criteria and instructions carefully.
4. Click on the 'Apply Online' link.
5. Fill in personal, educational, and contact details.
6. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and required documents.
7. Pay the application fee online (if applicable).
8. Submit the application and save the confirmation page.
9. Take a printout for future reference.

### Important Links

- [TNPSC Official Website](https://www.tnpsc.gov.in)
- [TN Velai Vaippu Portal](https://www.tnvelaivaaippu.gov.in)
- [Tamil Nadu Government Jobs Portal](https://www.tn.gov.in)

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

**Q1: What type of jobs are available in Tamil Nadu Government?**
A: TN Govt offers Group IV, Group II, VAO, Engineer, and other departmental posts like Apprentice, Assistant, Typist, Stenographer, Clerk, Driver, Manager, etc.

**Q2: Can other state candidates apply?**
A: Yes, but they must have knowledge of Tamil (Tamil as a subject in Matriculation/10th class).

**Q3: Is this page updated regularly?**
A: Yes, IndGovtJobs updates the TN Govt Jobs page every week with the latest notifications.

**Q4: What is the minimum qualification?**
A: Minimum 10th pass; higher qualifications required for specific posts.

**Q5: Is there any age relaxation?**
A: Yes, for OBC, SC, ST, PWD, and other reserved categories as per government rules.
