content:
# Diploma Government Jobs 2026 Recruitment: Over 9000 Vacancies!

Government job opportunities for diploma holders in 2026 are soaring, with more than 9000 vacancies announced across various Central and State Government departments and prominent Public Sector Units (PSUs) nationwide. This significant recruitment drive offers excellent career prospects for individuals holding diplomas in diverse engineering fields such as Electrical, Mechanical, Electronics, Civil, Computer Science, Electronics & Communication, and Chemical Engineering. Both freshers and experienced candidates are encouraged to apply for positions ranging from Junior Engineer to various technical and apprentice roles.

## Latest Vacancy Details for Diploma Holders – June 2026

Here's a snapshot of some of the latest government job opportunities for diploma holders:

| Post Names                       | Total Vacancies | Last Date    | Recruiting Body / Department       |
| :------------------------------- | :-------------- | :----------- | :--------------------------------- |
| Assistant Engineer, Junior Engineer | 09              | 14/08/2026   | Delhi Cantonment Board             |
| Junior Engineer Civil, Quality Assistant, Assistant CAMO, Trainee Technician | 20+             | 10/07/2026   | Pawan Hans Limited                 |
| Executive (Civil)                | 09              | 13/07/2026   | NEEPCO                             |
| Engineering Assistant Trainee    | 04              | 08/07/2026   | BEL Chennai Unit                   |
| Additional Assistant Engineer (Mechanical) | 110             | 29/06/2026   | GSSSB                              |
| Technician (Diploma) Apprentices | 160             | 25/06/2026   | Tamilnadu PWD                      |
| Junior Engineers                 | 175             | 14/07/2026   | NHSRCL                             |
| Various Technician Posts         | 100+            | 30/06/2026   | AIIMS CRE 5                        |
| Apprentice (Photography / Videography) | 02              | 01/07/2026   | CCRUM                              |
| Draughtsman, Electrician & Other Posts | 800+            | 04/07/2026   | Border Roads Organisation (BRO)    |
| Junior Technician                | 12              | 20/07/2026   | IIT Hyderabad                      |
| Technical / IT posts             | Various         | 15/07/2026   | DSSSB Advt No 03/2026              |
| Apprentices                      | Various         | 29/06/2026   | Indian Navy                        |
| Technician                       | 6565            | 29/07/2026   | Indian Railway (RRBs)              |
| UT, Divisional & District Cadre Posts | 10+             | 21/07/2026   | JKSSB                              |
| Junior Engineer, Architectural Assistant | 629             | 29/06/2026   | OSSC CTSRE                         |

*(Note: This is a partial list. For comprehensive details and additional vacancies, refer to the official notifications of respective departments/PSUs.)*

## Post Qualifications

For most diploma recruitment drives in public sector companies and government departments, the general educational qualification requirements are as follows:

*   Candidates must possess a three-year full-time diploma in an engineering discipline from an institution or university approved by AICTE or the Government.
*   Alternatively, candidates with a 10+2 qualification followed by a two-year diploma in an engineering discipline from AICTE or Government approved institutions/universities are also eligible.

## Age Limits

Age limits for various diploma government jobs vary significantly depending on the recruiting body, the specific post, and government reservation policies. Candidates are advised to consult the detailed official notification for each specific recruitment to ascertain the exact age criteria. Generally, age relaxation is provided for candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC/PWD categories as per government rules.

## Application Fee

The application fee for diploma government jobs is not uniform and varies based on the recruiting organization, the specific post, and the candidate's category. Candidates are required to check the official notification of the particular recruitment drive for precise details regarding application fees and acceptable payment methods.

## Exam Schedules & Important Dates

The deadlines for applications for various diploma government jobs are crucial. Below are some important 'Last Dates' mentioned in the latest notifications:

| Event             | Date (DD/MM/YYYY) |
| :---------------- | :---------------- |

| Last Date to Apply (Delhi Cantonment Board) | 14/08/2026        |

*(Note: Exam dates and further selection process timelines will be announced by the respective recruiting bodies. Candidates are advised to regularly check official websites for updates.)*

## How to Apply

Follow these steps to successfully apply for diploma government jobs:

1.  **Identify Opportunities:** Browse through the list of vacancies and identify the posts that match your qualifications and interests.
2.  **Refer Official Notification:** Download and thoroughly read the official recruitment notification for the chosen post from the respective government department or PSU's website.
3.  **Visit Official Portal:** Navigate to the official online application portal of the specific recruiting organization.
4.  **New Registration/Login:** If you are a new applicant, register by providing basic details such as name, email, and mobile number to generate login credentials. Existing users can log in directly.
5.  **Fill Application Form:** Complete the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and contact details. Ensure there are no errors.
6.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph, signature, diploma certificates, caste certificate (if applicable), and any other required documents in the specified format and size.
7.  **Pay Application Fee:** Pay the requisite application fee using the available online payment options (Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, UPI). Ensure to retain the e-receipt.
8.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the entered information before final submission. Make any necessary corrections.
9.  **Print Confirmation:** After successful submission, print a copy of the submitted application form and the e-receipt for your records and future reference.

## Category-wise Government Jobs for Diploma Holders

Diploma holders can find opportunities across various categories, including:

*   **FRESHER JOBS:** Opportunities specifically designed for candidates with no prior experience.
*   **RAILWAY JOBS:** Vacancies in various departments of Indian Railways.
*   **BANK SECTOR JOBS:** Roles in public sector banks.
*   **OFFICER JOBS:** Supervisory and officer-level positions.
*   **POLICE JOBS:** Technical roles within police departments.
*   **Metro Rail Vacancies:** Positions in urban metro rail projects.
*   **PUBLIC SECTOR (PSU) JOBS:** Recruitment in leading PSUs like BHEL, BEL, IOCL, ONGC, etc.
*   **IT SECTOR JOBS:** Roles requiring IT and computer science diplomas.
*   **APPRENTICE JOBS:** Training opportunities with a stipend.
*   **DEFENCE JOBS:** Technical roles in Indian Army, Navy, Air Force.
*   **STATE WISE JOBS:** Specific recruitments by state governments.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

### 1. What are the educational qualifications required for diploma recruitment in public sector companies?
The Candidates should possess a three-year full-time diploma or a 10+2 qualification followed by a two-year diploma in an engineering discipline from institutions or universities approved by AICTE or the Government.

### 2. Which government public sector companies recruit diploma holders?
The Companies like BHEL, BEL, Coal India, HPCL, EIL, BPCL, Mazagon Dock, MECON, NACL, NLC, NMDC, SAIL, NTPC, IOCL, ONGC, Power Grid, Railtel, RITES, and UCI are among those that recruit diploma holders.

### 3. In which engineering fields are diploma holders sought after by PSUs?
Diploma holders are sought in various engineering fields such as Electrical, Mechanical, Electronics, Civil, Computer Science, Electronics & Communication, and Chemical Engineering.

### 4. Are there opportunities for both fresher and experienced diploma holders in government jobs?
Yes, both Navaratna companies and other PSUs look for both freshers and experienced diploma holders for various positions.

### 5. Where can I find the latest job notifications for diploma holders in government sectors for 2026?
Check official websites of the respective PSUs, government job portals, employment news, and job notification websites for the latest updates on recruitment drives for 2026.

- Universities: Teacher, Clerk, Data Entry Operator.
- State Govt: Assistant, Clerk, Officer, Stenographer.

Check the table above for latest vacancies, last dates, and apply links. Click the green 'Govt Organization' link for full details.

B.Com is most in demand, followed by B.E./B.Tech, B.Sc, and BBA.

Bachelor's in Banking, Business Administration, Finance, IT, or Accounting.

### Selection Process

### Frequently Asked Questions
**Q: What type of law jobs are available in the government sector?**
A: Positions include Legal Assistants, Legal Officer, Legal Advisor, Insurance Law Officer, Judicial Services, District Judge, High Court Judge, Junior Consultant (Legal), Young Professional (Legal), and Manager Level - Legal Posts.

**Q: What are the qualifications for law jobs?**
A: Advocate Jobs require a Bachelor Degree in Law (BL/LLB) or Master Degree in Law (M.L.). After 12th, BA LLB or BBA LLB is also accepted.

**Q: Which sector provides maximum law jobs?**
A: The government sector provides the maximum number of law jobs, including District Courts, High Courts, Public Sector Companies, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government Universities, Public Sector Banks, Ministry of Railway, and UPSC.

**Q: What does LLB stand for?**
A: LLB stands for Legum Baccalaureus in Latin or Bachelor of Law Degree.

content:
## UP TET Admit Card 2026: Download Your Exam Details

The Uttar Pradesh Basic Education Board has officially released the Admit Cards for the Uttar Pradesh Teacher Eligibility Test (UP TET) 2026. This is a crucial update for all candidates who successfully registered for the examination. Candidates are strongly advised to visit the official UPBEB portal (once made available), log in with their credentials (Registration Number/Application Number and Password/Date of Birth), and download their admit cards immediately. The admit card is an indispensable document, containing vital information such as the candidate's name, photograph, roll number, exam date, shift timings, and most importantly, the allocated examination city and venue. Entry to the examination hall will be strictly denied to candidates who fail to present a valid printout of their admit card along with a photo identity proof. Ensure all details on your admit card are correct upon download.

### Important Dates

| Event                       | Date / Status   |
| :-------------------------- | :-------------- |
| UP TET 2026 Application Period | To be updated   |
| Admit Card Release          | Available Now   |
| UP TET 2026 Exam Date       | To be announced |
| UP TET 2026 Result          | To be announced |

### Verification Details on Your Admit Card

Upon downloading, candidates must meticulously check the following details on their UP TET 2026 Admit Card:
*   Candidate's Name
*   Father's/Mother's Name
*   Date of Birth
*   Roll Number
*   Application Number
*   Photograph and Signature
*   Examination Centre Name and Address
*   Exam Date and Reporting Time
*   Instructions for the examination

In case of any discrepancy in the details, candidates should immediately contact the Uttar Pradesh Basic Education Board or the relevant examination authority for rectification before the exam date.

### Documents Required on Exam Day

Candidates appearing for the UP TET 2026 examination must carry the following essential documents to the examination center without fail:
*   **Printed copy of UP TET 2026 Admit Card:** A clear and legible printout of the downloaded admit card. Digital copies are not permitted.
*   **Original Photo Identity Proof:** A valid, government-issued photo identification card such as an Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Passport, or Driving License. The identity proof must match the details on the admit card. Photocopies or scanned copies will not be accepted.
*   **Two Passport-size Photographs:** These should ideally be the same as those uploaded during the application process. These might be required for attendance sheets or other verification purposes at the center.

### Exam Day Guidelines

All candidates are expected to strictly adhere to the following rules and guidelines on the day of the examination to ensure a fair and smooth process:
*   Arrive at the examination center well before the reporting time mentioned on your admit card to complete pre-exam formalities such as biometric verification and frisking.
*   Do not carry any prohibited items such as mobile phones, smartwatches, electronic gadgets, books, notes, loose papers, or any other unauthorized material into the examination hall. Any such item will lead to disqualification.
*   Follow all instructions and directives given by the invigilators and examination staff strictly.
*   Maintain discipline and decorum within the examination premises. Any form of malpractice or misbehavior will result in immediate disqualification.
*   Candidates must sign the attendance sheet in the presence of the invigilator.

### Important Links

*   **Official Website of Uttar Pradesh Basic Education Board:** [To be updated]
*   **Direct Link to Download UP TET 2026 Admit Card:** [To be updated on official website]

Employment News is a weekly newspaper that lists government job vacancies, admission notices, exam schedules, and results.

content:
## RRB NTPC Undergraduate Answer Key 2026 - CEN 07/2025 CBT 1

The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has released the provisional answer key for the RRB NTPC Undergraduate (CEN 07/2025) CBT Stage 1 on 27 June 2026. Candidates can download the answer key and response sheet from the official RRB websites.

### Important Dates
| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Answer Key Release | 27 June 2026 (7 PM) |
| Objection Window | 28 June to 2 July 2026 |
| Final Result | To be announced |

### Marking Scheme
- Each correct answer: +1 mark
- Each incorrect answer: -1/3 mark (negative marking)
- Unanswered: 0 marks

### How to Download Answer Key
1. Visit the official RRB website of your region.
2. Click on the link for 'RRB NTPC Undergraduate Answer Key 2026'.
3. Enter your registration number and date of birth.
4. Download the answer key and response sheet PDF.

### Objection Process
- Candidates can raise objections online from 28 June to 2 July 2026.
- Objection fee: ₹100 per question (refundable if objection is accepted).
- Provide valid supporting documents for each objection.

### Score Calculation
- Calculate your raw score using the formula: (Number of correct answers) - (1/3 × Number of incorrect answers).
- Compare with the expected cut-off for your category.

For more details, visit the official RRB website.

title: Graduate Govt Jobs 2026 - 25000+ Vacancies Opening
overview: The year 2026 brings a massive wave of government job opportunities for graduates across India. With over 25,000 vacancies announced by various central government departments, public sector undertakings, banks, railways, and state-level recruitments, this is a golden chance for BA, B.Sc, B.Com, and other degree holders to secure a stable and prestigious career. The vacancies span multiple sectors including banking, railways, defence, PSUs, and more. Key recruiters include UPSC, SSC, RRB, State Bank of India, and various state public service commissions. Candidates are advised to check eligibility, apply before deadlines, and prepare for upcoming exams.
eligibility: Bachelor's Degree (BA, B.Sc, B.Com or equivalent) from a recognized university. Specific posts may require additional qualifications as per notification.
age_limit: 18 to 30 years (varies by post; relaxations as per government norms)
salary: Varies by post; typically ranges from ₹25,000 to ₹1,00,000 per month
how_to_apply:
1. Visit the official website of the respective recruitment body (e.g., upsc.gov.in, ssc.nic.in, rrbcdg.gov.in).
2. Read the detailed notification and check eligibility.
3. Click on the 'Apply Online' link for the desired post.
4. Register with a valid email and phone number.
5. Fill in the application form with personal, educational, and other details.
6. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and required documents.
7. Pay the application fee (if applicable) online.
8. Submit the form and take a printout for future reference.
meta_title: Graduate Govt Jobs 2026 - 25000+ Vacancies Opening 2026: Online Form, Eligibility & Benefits
meta_description: The year 2026 brings a massive wave of government job opportunities for graduates across India. With over 25,000 vacancies announced by various central gov
content:
## Graduate Govt Jobs 2026 - 25000+ Vacancies Opening

### Overview
Candidates who have completed BA, B.Sc, B.Com or any 3-Year Degree (UG) and are searching for Government Jobs in India can find the latest and upcoming Graduate Govt Job vacancies here. Central Government, State Government, Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) and Government organizations regularly recruit Degree holders for various posts across India.

### Application Fee
| Category | Fee (₹) |
|----------|---------|
| General / OBC | 500 - 1000 (varies by post) |
| SC / ST / PwD | 0 - 250 (varies by post) |
| Female (some states) | 0 - 250 |

### Important Dates
| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Application Start | June 2026 |
| Last Date for Applications | Varies (see table below) |
| Exam Date | To be announced |

### Vacancy Details

### How to Apply
1. Visit the official website of the respective recruitment body.
2. Read the detailed notification and check eligibility.
3. Click on the 'Apply Online' link for the desired post.
4. Register with a valid email and phone number.
5. Fill in the application form with personal, educational, and other details.
6. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and required documents.
7. Pay the application fee (if applicable) online.
8. Submit the form and take a printout for future reference.

title: Maharashtra Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana
department: Women and Child Development Department, Government of Maharashtra
overview: The scheme aims to empower women from economically weaker sections by providing direct financial assistance. It provides an annual benefit of Rs. 6,000 to eligible women aged 21-60 years.
eligibility: Women residents of Maharashtra, aged 21-60 years, belonging to families with annual income less than Rs. 2.5 lakh.
age_limit: 21-60 years
salary: Rs. 6,000 per year
how_to_apply: Visit the official website, register with Aadhaar and bank details, submit the application form online or at the nearest Anganwadi center.
meta_title: Maharashtra Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana 2026: Online Form, Eligibility & Benefits
meta_description: The scheme aims to empower women from economically weaker sections by providing direct financial assistance. It provides an annual benefit of Rs. 6,000 to
content:
### Benefits
- Direct cash transfer of Rs. 6,000 per year to eligible women.
- Financial independence and support for daily needs.

### Documents Required
- Aadhaar card
- Income certificate
- Residence proof
- Bank account details
- Age proof

### How to Apply
1. Visit the official website.
2. Click on 'Apply Now' and fill in personal details.
3. Upload required documents.
4. Submit the form and note the application number.

title: Rajasthan Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana
department: Medical and Health Department, Government of Rajasthan
overview: The scheme provides cashless health insurance coverage up to Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care. It aims to reduce out-of-pocket healthcare expenses for the poor.
eligibility: All families listed in the Socio-Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011 database of Rajasthan.
age_limit: N/A
salary: Health insurance cover up to Rs. 5 lakh per family per year
how_to_apply: Beneficiaries are automatically enrolled based on SECC data. For new applications, visit the nearest e-Mitra center or the official website.
meta_title: Rajasthan Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana 2026: Online Form, Eligibility & Benefits
meta_description: The scheme provides cashless health insurance coverage up to Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care. It aims to reduce out-of-pocke
content:
### Benefits
- Cashless treatment at empaneled hospitals.
- Coverage for pre-existing diseases from day one.
- Includes maternity benefits.

### Documents Required
- Aadhaar card
- SECC certificate (if available)
- Family photo

### How to Apply
1. Check eligibility on the official website.
2. Visit an e-Mitra center with documents.
3. Fill the application form and submit.
4. Receive a Chiranjeevi card.

title: Rythu Bandhu Scheme
overview: The scheme provides investment support of Rs. 5,000 per acre per season to farmers for crop cultivation. It aims to reduce input costs and ensure timely availability of funds.

salary: Rs. 5,000 per acre per season (Rs. 10,000 per acre per year)
how_to_apply: Beneficiaries are identified through land records. Farmers can check their eligibility on the official website and update bank details if needed.

overview: The Delhi Annashree Yojana is a scheme to provide financial assistance to poor families in Delhi, supporting them in purchasing food grains. The scheme aims to reduce poverty and hunger. It offers a monthly financial assistance of Rs. 600.
eligibility: Poor families, with a monthly income of less than Rs. 10,000, are eligible for the scheme.
age_limit: N/A
salary: Monthly financial assistance of Rs. 600
how_to_apply: Families can apply for the scheme through the official website or by visiting the nearest food and civil supplies office. They need to fill the application form and submit the required documents.

content:
## Law Government Jobs 2026 - Overview

The government sector offers a wide range of law jobs for LLB, BL, and LLM graduates, as well as advocates. Positions are available in High Courts, District Courts, Public Sector Undertakings, and central/state government departments. This recruitment drive includes over 500 vacancies for roles such as Assistant District Prosecution Officer, Legal Advisor, Law Officer, and more. Eligible candidates can apply through the respective official portals before the deadlines.

### Application Fee

| Category | Fee (₹) |
|----------|---------|
| General / OBC | 500 - 1000 (varies by post) |
| SC / ST / PWD | 0 - 250 (varies by post) |
| All Female | 0 - 250 (varies by post) |

*Note: Fee details may vary for each recruitment. Refer to the official notification.*

### Important Dates

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Application Start Date | Varies by post |
| Last Date to Apply | 21 July 2026 (for some posts) |
| Exam Date | To be announced |
| Admit Card Release | To be announced |

### Vacancy Details

| Post Name | Total Vacancies | Last Date |
|-----------|----------------|-----------|
| Assistant District Prosecution Officer | 15 | 21/07/2026 |
| Executive (Law) - CGPSC | 01 | 13/07/2026 |
| Young Professional (Legal) - NEEPCO | 04 | 04/07/2026 |
| Manager (Legal) - NFRA | 01 | 01/07/2026 |
| Assistant Legal Adviser, Assistant Public Prosecutor - UPMSCL | 17 | 03/07/2026 |
| Manager / Chief Manager (Corporate Legal) - UPSC Advt No.06.2026 | 02 | 06/07/2026 |
| Assistant Prosecution Officer - Mumbai Port Authority | 371 | 07/07/2026 |
| Law, Justice and Parliamentary Affairs Department - RPSC | 01 | 21/07/2026 |
| Law Officer - JKSSB | Not specified | 06/07/2026 |
| Law Officer - AIIMS Deoghar | Not specified | Not specified |

### Eligibility Criteria

- **Educational Qualification:** Bachelor of Laws (LLB), Bachelor of Law (BL), or Master of Laws (LLM) from a recognized university.
- **Age Limit:** Varies by post; typically 18-35 years. Age relaxation as per government rules.
- **Experience:** Some posts require prior legal practice or experience.

### Salary Structure

- Pay Level 6 to Level 10 (approx. ₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400 per month) plus allowances as per government norms.

### How to Apply

1. Visit the official website of the respective recruiting body.
2. Read the detailed notification and check eligibility.
3. Click on the application link for the desired post.
4. Fill in personal, educational, and contact details.
5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and required documents.
6. Pay the application fee (if applicable) online.
7. Submit the application and take a printout for future reference.

### Selection Process

- Written Exam (Objective/Descriptive)
- Interview / Document Verification
- Medical Examination (if applicable)

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**Q: What type of law jobs are available in the Government Sector?**
A: Various positions such as Legal Assistants, Legal Officer, Legal Advisor, Insurance Law Officer, Judicial Services, District Judge, High Court Judge, Junior Consultant (Legal), Young Professional (Legal), and Manager Level - Legal Posts.

**Q: What are the qualifications for law jobs?**
A: Bachelor Degree in Law (BL), LLB (Bachelor of Legislative Law), Master Degree in Law (M.L.), or integrated courses like BA LLB, BBA LLB after 12th.

**Q: Which sector provides maximum number of law jobs?**
A: Government Sector provides maximum law jobs for Law Graduates, eligible Advocates, and LLB candidates. Sectors include District Courts, High Courts, Public Sector Companies, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government Universities, Public Sector Banks, Railways, and UPSC.

**Q: What does LLB stand for?**
A: LLB stands for Legum Baccalaureus in Latin or Bachelor of Law Degree.

*For more details, refer to the official notification of each recruitment.*

The selection will be based on a written exam and/or interview as per PMC norms.

content:
### UPPSC PCS Pre 2026 Recruitment Notification: Await Official Release

The Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC) is set to announce the Combined State/Upper Subordinate Services (PCS) Preliminary Examination 2026. This notification opens doors for aspiring candidates to join various esteemed positions within the Uttar Pradesh state government. While the full details are still awaited with the official notification, this page will provide comprehensive information as soon as it is released. Candidates are strongly encouraged to regularly check the official UPPSC website for the most accurate and up-to-date information regarding this significant recruitment drive.

#### Important Dates & Exam Schedule

The detailed schedule for the UPPSC PCS Pre 2026 Examination, including application start and end dates, admit card release, and preliminary exam dates, will be published along with the official notification.

| Event                         | Date (Tentative/To Be Announced) |
| :---------------------------- | :------------------------------- |
| Notification Release Date     | To Be Announced                  |
| Application Start Date        | To Be Announced                  |
| Application End Date          | To Be Announced                  |
| Last Date for Fee Payment     | To Be Announced                  |
| Admit Card Release Date       | To Be Announced                  |
| Preliminary Exam Date         | To Be Announced                  |
| Preliminary Exam Result Date  | To Be Announced                  |

#### Application Fee

The application fee structure for the UPPSC PCS Pre 2026 Examination will be detailed in the official notification. Candidates will typically be able to pay the fee through online modes such as Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, or E-Challan.

| Category                      | Fee Amount (Tentative/To Be Announced) |
| :---------------------------- | :------------------------------------- |
| General/OBC/EWS               | To Be Announced                        |
| SC/ST                         | To Be Announced                        |
| PwD (Divyangjan)              | To Be Announced                        |
| Ex-Servicemen                 | To Be Announced                        |

#### Vacancy Details (Post-wise)

The exact number of vacancies for various posts under the Combined State/Upper Subordinate Services Examination 2026 will be specified in the official notification. Candidates should refer to the detailed advertisement for a comprehensive list of posts and their respective vacancy counts.

*   **Total Vacancies:** To Be Announced
*   **Specific Posts:** To Be Announced (e.g., Deputy Collector, Deputy Superintendent of Police, Block Development Officer, etc.)

#### Eligibility Criteria

Detailed eligibility criteria, including educational qualifications and age limits, will be outlined in the official UPPSC PCS Pre 2026 notification.

##### 1. Educational Qualification

*   **General Requirement:** Candidates must possess a Bachelor's Degree from any recognized University or an equivalent qualification up to the last date for receipt of the online application.
*   **Specific Post Qualifications:** For certain specialized posts, specific educational qualifications will be required. These details will be elaborated in the official notification.

##### 2. Age Limit (as on a specific cut-off date, usually July 1st of the application year)

*   **Minimum Age:** To Be Announced (Typically 21 years)
*   **Maximum Age:** To Be Announced (Typically 40 years for Unreserved category)
*   **Age Relaxation:** Category-wise age relaxation will be applicable as per Uttar Pradesh Government rules. Details will be provided in the official notification.

#### How to Apply for UPPSC PCS Pre 2026

Once the official notification and application portal are active, eligible candidates can apply online by following these steps:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official website of the Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC) - `uppsc.up.nic.in`.
2.  **Locate Recruitment Section:** Navigate to the 'Recruitment' or 'All Notifications/Advertisements' section.
3.  **Find the Notification:** Click on the link for 'Combined State/Upper Subordinate Services (PCS) Examination 2026 Notification'.
4.  **Read Official Notification:** Download and thoroughly read the official detailed notification to understand all instructions, eligibility criteria, and important dates.
5.  **New Registration:** If you are a new user, complete the One Time Registration (OTR) process or register by providing basic details such as name, email, and mobile number.
6.  **Fill Application Form:** Log in using your registration credentials and fill out the online application form with all required personal, educational, and other details.
7.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and any other required documents in the specified format and size.
8.  **Pay Application Fee:** Pay the applicable application fee through the online payment gateway.
9.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the entered information before final submission. Make sure there are no errors.
10. **Print Application:** After successful submission, print a copy of the filled application form and fee receipt for your records.

#### Important Links

*   **Official Website:** [https://uppsc.up.nic.in/](https://uppsc.up.nic.in/)
*   **Download Official Notification (Will be updated upon release):** To Be Updated
*   **Apply Online (Will be updated upon release):** To Be Updated

content:
# UP Police Constable 2026 Admit Card: Essential Details

The Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB) is preparing to issue the Admit Cards for the Constable Recruitment Examination 2026. This is a crucial step for all aspirants vying for the 32,679 Constable positions within the Uttar Pradesh Police force. Candidates are strongly advised to download their admit cards promptly upon release and adhere to all instructions provided to ensure a smooth examination experience.

## Important Dates

Stay informed about the key timelines related to the UP Police Constable 2026 examination and admit card release:

| Event                      | Date (Tentative)          |
| :------------------------- | :------------------------ |
| Application Period         | To be Announced           |
| Admit Card Release Date    | Expected Mid-December 2025 |
| Written Examination Date   | January/February 2026     |
| Result Declaration         | To be Announced           |

*Note: Dates are tentative and subject to change by the UPPRPB. Candidates are advised to regularly check the official website for the latest updates.*

## Exam Day Guidelines and Instructions

Candidates must strictly follow the guidelines issued by UPPRPB for the examination. Failure to comply may lead to disqualification.

*   **Arrival Time:** Report to the examination center well in advance of the stipulated reporting time mentioned on your admit card to complete pre-exam formalities.
*   **Admit Card:** A printed copy of the Admit Card is mandatory for entry. Without it, candidates will not be permitted to take the exam.
*   **Valid Photo ID:** Along with the admit card, candidates must carry one original valid photo identification proof (e.g., Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Driving License, Passport).
*   **Prohibited Items:** Do not bring electronic gadgets such as mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, or any study material into the examination hall. Any such item found will lead to immediate disqualification.
*   **Biometric Verification:** Be prepared for biometric verification at the exam center.
*   **Rough Work:** All rough work must be done only on the sheets provided by the invigilator.
*   **COVID-19 Protocols:** Candidates may be required to follow specific health and safety protocols, including wearing masks and maintaining social distancing, as per prevailing government guidelines.

## Documents to Carry on Exam Day

To ensure a hassle-free entry and verification process at the examination center, candidates must carry the following documents:

1.  **Printed Copy of UP Police Constable 2026 Admit Card.**
2.  **Original Valid Photo Identity Proof:** (Any one of the following: Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID Card, Driving License, Passport).
3.  **Two recent passport-sized photographs** (preferably the same as uploaded in the application form).

## Important Links

*   **Official UPPRPB Website:** [https://uppbpb.gov.in/](https://uppbpb.gov.in/)
*   **Admit Card Download (Direct Link):** (Link will be activated on the official UPPRPB website upon release)

content:
## UP Board Class 10th & 12th Result 2026 Announcement

The Uttar Pradesh Madhyamik Shiksha Parishad (UPMSP) is set to declare the results for the Class 10th (High School) and Class 12th (Intermediate) examinations for the year 2026. This is a highly anticipated event for students who appeared in these prestigious board exams across Uttar Pradesh. Students can check their results directly through the official UPMSP result portal using their unique roll numbers.

### Important Dates for Result Declaration

Stay informed about the key timelines related to the UP Board Result 2026:

| Event                      | Date                  |
| :------------------------- | :-------------------- |
| Class 10th Result (High School) | To be Announced (Expected May 2026) |
| Class 12th Result (Intermediate) | To be Announced (Expected May 2026) |
| Provisional Marksheet Download | Immediately after result declaration |
| Original Marksheet & Certificate Distribution | To be announced by respective schools |

### How to Check Your UP Board Result 2026

Follow these simple steps to access your UP Board Class 10th or 12th result quickly and efficiently:

1.  **Visit the Official Portal:** Navigate to the official UP Board result website, `upresults.nic.in`.
2.  **Locate Result Link:** On the homepage, find and click on the specific link for 'UP Board High School (Class 10th) Result 2026' or 'UP Board Intermediate (Class 12th) Result 2026'.
3.  **Enter Credentials:** A new page will appear where you need to enter your unique Roll Number, exactly as it appears on your examination admit card.
4.  **Submit and View:** After entering the Roll Number, click on the 'Submit' or 'View Result' button.
5.  **Download Provisional Marksheet:** Your result will be displayed on the screen. It is highly recommended to download and print your provisional mark sheet for immediate reference and future academic applications.

### Details Required to Check Result

To check your UP Board result, you will primarily need your:

*   **Roll Number:** This is the most crucial piece of information, available on your admit card.

### Post-Result Guidelines

*   **Provisional Mark Sheet:** The online result is a provisional mark sheet. The original mark sheets and passing certificates will be issued by the respective schools a few weeks after the online declaration.
*   **Verification:** Students are advised to carefully check all details on their provisional mark sheet, including their name, parent's name, subjects, and marks. In case of any discrepancies, contact your school authorities immediately.
*   **Re-evaluation/Scrutiny:** Information regarding re-evaluation or scrutiny of answer sheets will be released by UPMSP shortly after the results. Students dissatisfied with their marks can apply for these processes as per the board's guidelines.

content:
# Indian Defence & Police Sector Recruitment 2026

The Ministry of Defence (MOD), Government of India, along with various Indian Armed Forces and State Police Departments, has announced a significant recruitment drive for 2026, offering over 1000 government vacancies. This is a golden opportunity for individuals seeking a fulfilling career in national service, covering a wide range of roles from Non-Executive positions to Officer and Apprentice posts.

## Vacancy Details

Here's a list of some of the latest and upcoming defence and police job notifications:

| Post Name / Details                     | Department                       | Total Vacancies | Closing Date |
| :-------------------------------------- | :------------------------------- | :-------------- | :----------- |
| Non Executive (ITI)                     | BEML Limited                     | 362             | 10/07/2026   |
| Apprentice Posts                        | DRDO DESIDOC                     | 20              | 01/07/2026   |
| Agniveervayu                            | Indian Air Force                 | -               | 26/07/2026   |
| Group Y Airmen Medical Assistant        | Indian Air Force                 | -               | 26/07/2026   |
| SSC Officers                            | Indian Navy                      | 275             | 27/07/2026   |
| Group ‘C’ Posts                         | Border Roads Organisation (BRO)  | 899             | 04/07/2026   |
| Paid Internship                         | DEAL Dehradun                    | 45              | 30/06/2026   |
| Research Posts                          | INMAS DRDO                       | 15              | 30/06/2026   |
| Agniveer (Apprentice) Posts             | Indian Navy                      | -               | 29/06/2026   |

*Note: 'Total Vacancies' for Agniveer posts are not specified in the raw text but are part of the larger recruitment drive.* 

## Application Fee

The application fee varies depending on the specific post, department, and category of the applicant. Candidates are advised to consult the respective official notification for exact fee details.

| Category               | Application Fee (Tentative) |
| :--------------------- | :-------------------------- |
| General/OBC/EWS        | Refer Official Notification |
| SC/ST/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen | Refer Official Notification |
| Female Candidates      | Refer Official Notification |

## Important Dates

Candidates should keep track of the following crucial dates for various recruitments. Please note that these are closing dates for specific posts mentioned above. Individual notifications will have more detailed timelines.

| Event                       | Date         |
| :-------------------------- | :----------- |
| Application Closing Date    | Various      |
| Latest Closing Date         | 27/07/2026   |

## Post-wise Qualifications

The eligibility criteria for these diverse roles span various educational backgrounds. Candidates should ensure they meet the specific requirements for their chosen post.

*   **For 10th Pass/12th Pass:** Many entry-level positions, including certain roles in the Police Departments and Armed Forces (e.g., Agniveer schemes, Group 'C' posts), require candidates to have successfully completed their 10th or 12th standard education from a recognized board.
*   **For ITI/Diploma Holders:** Technical roles, such as Non-Executive positions in organizations like BEML or specific technical trades in the Armed Forces, often require an Industrial Training Institute (ITI) certification or a Diploma in a relevant engineering discipline.
*   **For Graduates:** Officer level posts, Research positions (e.g., DRDO, INMAS), and various other administrative or specialized roles demand a Bachelor's Degree in the relevant field from a recognized university. This includes qualifications like B.E./B.Tech for Engineering Graduates, MBA, MCA, and Law Graduates for specific roles.

## Age Limits

Age limits are determined by the recruiting agency and the specific nature of the post. Generally, the age criteria for Defence and Police jobs range from 18 years to 30 years, with relaxations provided for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen) as per Government of India norms. It is imperative to check the detailed official notification for the exact age requirements for each specific vacancy you intend to apply for.

content:
## Law Government Job Opportunities 2026: Apply Now for 500+ Vacancies!

The Indian Government sector is opening doors for legal professionals with over 500 vacancies in various Central and State Government Departments, Public Sector Undertakings, and the Judiciary. This is a golden chance for Law Graduates, Advocates, LLB, and BL degree holders, both freshers and experienced, to secure esteemed positions and contribute to the nation's legal framework. From Assistant Prosecution Officers to Legal Managers and Advisers, a multitude of roles await aspiring candidates.

### Vacancy Details

Here's a glimpse of some of the latest law-related government job openings:

| Name of Post | Total Vacancies | Last Date | Department/Body |
| :----------------------------------------------- | :---------------- | :---------------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Assistant District Prosecution Officer | 15 | 21/07/2026 | Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission (CGPSC) |
| Executive (Law) | 01 | 13/07/2026 | Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission (CGPSC) |
| Young Professional (Legal) | 04 | 04/07/2026 | North Eastern Electric Power Corporation (NEEPCO) |
| Manager (Legal) | 01 | 01/07/2026 | National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) |
| Assistant Legal Adviser, Assistant Public Prosecutor | 17 | 03/07/2026 | Uttar Pradesh Medical Supplies Corporation Ltd (UPMSCL) |
| Manager / Chief Manager (Corporate Legal) | 02 | 06/07/2026 | Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) |
| Assistant Prosecution Officer | 371 | 07/07/2026 | Mumbai Port Authority |
| Law, Justice and Parliamentary Affairs Department | 01 | 21/07/2026 | Rajasthan Public Service Commission (RPSC) |
| Law Officer | N/A (Refer Notification) | 06/07/2026 | Jammu & Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB) |

*Note: Vacancy numbers and last dates are subject to change. Candidates are advised to refer to the official notification for the most accurate and up-to-date information for each specific post.*

### Application Fee

The application fee varies for each specific recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to refer to the detailed official notification of the respective recruiting body for exact fee amounts, payment methods, and any applicable fee exemptions for reserved categories.

### Important Dates / Exam Schedules

Keep track of these crucial dates to ensure you don't miss any deadlines:

| Event | Date |
| :-------------------------- | :----------------------------------- |
| Application Start Date | To be announced for individual posts |
| Last Date for Application | Varies by post (Latest: 21/07/2026) |
| Admit Card Release | To be announced |
| Written Exam / Interview Date | To be announced |
| Result Declaration | To be announced |

### Post-wise Qualifications

To be eligible for various law government jobs, candidates typically need the following qualifications:

*   **Educational Qualification:**
    *   Bachelor Degree in Law (BL)
    *   Legum Baccalaureus or LLB (Bachelor of Legislative Law)
    *   Master Degree in Law (M.L.)
    *   Integrated courses like BA LLB, BBA LLB after 12th class.

*Specific educational qualifications, experience, and other requirements (e.g., bar registration) will be detailed in the individual job notifications.*

### Age Limits

Age criteria vary significantly depending on the specific government department and the nature of the post. Generally, the age limit is between 18 to 30 or 35 years, with considerable relaxations for candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), Other Backward Classes (OBC), Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD), and Ex-Servicemen as per Government of India rules. Always refer to the specific job notification for precise age limits and relaxation policies.

### How to Apply

Candidates interested in these opportunities must apply online through the official websites of the respective recruiting organizations. Follow these general steps:

1.  **Identify the Post:** Select the specific law job opening for which you meet the eligibility criteria.
2.  **Access Official Notification:** Download and thoroughly read the official detailed notification from the recruitment body's website.
3.  **Online Registration:** If you are a new user, register on the portal by providing basic details to generate a unique registration ID and password.
4.  **Fill Application Form:** Log in with your credentials and accurately fill out the online application form with all required personal, educational, and professional details.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable), and any other relevant documents as per the prescribed format and size.
6.  **Pay Application Fee:** Proceed to pay the application fee, if applicable, through the provided online payment modes.
7.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the entered information before final submission. No changes may be allowed after submission.
8.  **Print Application:** After successful submission, download and print a copy of your application form and e-receipt for your records.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

#### ✅ What type of law jobs are available in the Government Sector?

Different types of Law job positions are available in the Govt Sector, such as:

*   Legal Assistants
*   Legal Officer
*   Legal Advisor
*   Insurance Law Officer
*   Judicial Services
*   District Judge
*   High Court Judge
*   Junior Consultant (Legal)
*   Young Professional (Legal)
*   Manager Level - Legal Posts

#### ✅ What are the qualifications for law jobs?

Advocate Jobs Qualifications typically include:

*   Bachelor Degree in Law (BL)
*   Legum Baccalaureus or LLB (Bachelor of Legislative Law)
*   Master Degree in Law (M.L.)
*   Integrated courses like BA LLB, BBA LLB after 12th class

#### ✅ Which sector provides the maximum number of law jobs?

Government Sector provides a maximum number of Law jobs for Law Graduates, eligible Advocates, and LLB candidates. The Law Jobs providing Govt Sectors are:

*   District Court Judicial Services
*   High Court Judicial Services
*   Public Sector Companies
*   Ministry of Law and Justice
*   Government Universities / Colleges - Maharashtra National Law University (MNLU), Indian Law Institute (ILI), Pt Motilal Nehru Law College etc.
*   Public Sector Banks
*   Ministry of Railway
*   Union Public Service Commission (UPSC)

#### ✅ What does LLB stand for?

LLB stands for Legum Baccalaureus in Latin or Bachelor of Law Degree.

content:
## India Government Mint Admit Card 2026 Released

The India Government Mint has officially released the admit cards for the **Supervisor, Junior Office Assistant, Junior Technician & More Posts** under its 2026 recruitment drive. This release is a critical step for all eligible candidates preparing to appear for the upcoming examination. It is imperative that all applicants download their admit cards without delay, as it is a mandatory document for entry into the examination center.

### Important Dates

To keep candidates informed, here are the key dates related to the admit card release and examination:

| Event                  | Date                              |
| :--------------------- | :-------------------------------- |
| Admit Card Release Date| Released                          |
| Examination Date       | To be announced / As per Admit Card |
| Last Date to Download  | Refer Official Notice             |


### Posts Covered

This admit card release pertains to various significant roles within the India Government Mint, providing opportunities in a range of technical and administrative fields. The posts for which admit cards have been issued include:

*   Supervisor
*   Junior Office Assistant
*   Junior Technician
*   And Other Posts (as specified in the original recruitment notification)


### Eligibility for Admit Card Download

Only those candidates who successfully registered and met all the eligibility criteria specified in the initial recruitment notification are eligible to download their admit cards. Access to the admit card download portal will require your unique registration number and password (or Date of Birth) that was generated during the application process.

### Exam Day Guidelines

Candidates appearing for the India Government Mint examination must adhere to the following guidelines to ensure a fair and organized testing environment:

1.  **Admit Card:** A clear printout of your downloaded admit card is essential. Entry without a valid admit card will be denied.
2.  **Photo Identity Proof:** Bring one original, valid photo identification proof (e.g., Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Driving License, Passport). The details on the ID must match those on the admit card.
3.  **Reporting Time:** Arrive at the examination center well in advance of the reporting time indicated on your admit card to complete necessary verification procedures.
4.  **Prohibited Items:** Do not carry any electronic devices (mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators), study materials, or any other unauthorized items into the examination hall.
5.  **Instructions:** Carefully read and follow all instructions provided by the examination invigilators and those printed on your admit card.
6.  **COVID-19 Protocols:** Follow any prevailing health and safety protocols, including wearing a mask and maintaining social distancing, if mandated by the authorities.

### Documents Required for Verification

Candidates should be prepared to present the following documents for verification at the examination center:

*   Printed copy of the India Government Mint Admit Card 2026.
*   Original Photo Identity Proof (e.g., Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Driving License, Passport).
*   Two recent passport-sized photographs (preferably the same as uploaded during the application).

### How to Download India Government Mint Admit Card 2026

Follow these step-by-step instructions to successfully download your admit card:

1.  **Step 1: Visit the Official Website:** Navigate to the official careers portal of Security Printing and Minting Corporation of India Limited (SPMCIL) or the specific India Government Mint website (e.g., `spmcil.com/careers` or the respective mint's career section).
2.  **Step 2: Locate the Admit Card Link:** On the homepage, look for the 'Careers', 'Latest Updates', or 'Recruitment' section. Find and click on the link titled 'Admit Card for Supervisor, Junior Office Assistant, Junior Technician & More Posts 2026' or similar.
3.  **Step 3: Enter Login Credentials:** You will be redirected to a login page. Enter your Registration Number/Roll Number and Password (or Date of Birth) as per your application details.
4.  **Step 4: Verify and Download:** After entering the required information, click on 'Submit' or 'Login'. Your admit card will be displayed on the screen. Carefully check all the details, including your name, photograph, exam date, time, and venue.
5.  **Step 5: Print Your Admit Card:** Download the admit card in PDF format and take at least two clear printouts. Keep one copy safely for future reference.

In case of any discrepancy in your admit card details, immediately contact the India Government Mint examination authorities for rectification before the exam date.

content:
## RRB NTPC Undergraduate Answer Key 2026 (CEN 07/2025 CBT 1) Released

Good news for candidates who appeared for the Railway Recruitment Board's (RRB) Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) Undergraduate Computer Based Test 1 (CEN 07/2025)! The provisional answer key for CBT 1 has been officially released, allowing aspirants to cross-check their responses and estimate their scores. This is a critical stage for all participants as it provides transparency in the evaluation process and an opportunity to challenge any discrepancies.

### Important Dates

Candidates should make a note of the following important date:

| Event | Date & Time |
| :---------------------------- | :------------------ |
| RRB NTPC Undergraduate Answer Key Release | 27 June 2026 (7 PM) |
| Objection Window Start Date | To be announced soon |
| Objection Window End Date | To be announced soon |

### How to Download RRB NTPC CEN 07/2025 CBT 1 Answer Key

Follow these simple steps to access your provisional answer key and response sheet:

1.  **Visit Official RRB Website:** Navigate to the official website of the Railway Recruitment Board zone from which you applied.
2.  **Locate Answer Key Link:** On the homepage, search for the direct link titled 'RRB NTPC CEN 07/2025 CBT 1 Answer Key' or 'Response Sheet Download'.
3.  **Enter Login Credentials:** Click on the link and you will be redirected to the login page. Enter your Registration Number and Date of Birth (as per your application form).
4.  **View Answer Key:** After successful login, your provisional answer key, along with your marked responses, will be displayed on the screen.
5.  **Download & Print:** Download the PDF of your answer key and response sheet. It is advisable to take a printout for offline review and score calculation.

### Objection Submission Process & Fee Details

In case candidates find any errors or discrepancies in the provisional answer key, the RRB provides an opportunity to raise objections. Here's a general outline of the process:

*   **Access Objection Portal:** After logging in to view your answer key, locate the 'Objection Tracker' or 'Raise Objection' link.
*   **Identify Questions:** Select the specific question numbers against which you wish to submit an objection.
*   **Provide Justification:** Clearly state your reason for the objection and upload supporting documents or references (e.g., from standard textbooks, official sources) to substantiate your claim.
*   **Objection Fee:** A prescribed fee is usually charged per objection. Candidates must pay this fee online through the provided payment gateway. The exact fee details will be specified in the official notification regarding the answer key challenge. Please refer to the official notification for precise fee amounts and payment methods.
*   **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the details before final submission. Only objections submitted with valid proof and the required fee will be considered.

### Understanding the Marking Scheme and Score Calculation

To calculate your probable score from the provisional answer key, it's crucial to understand the marking scheme used in RRB NTPC CBT 1:

*   **Positive Marks:** For every correct answer, candidates will be awarded the specified marks (typically 1 mark).
*   **Negative Marks:** There is usually a negative marking for incorrect answers (e.g., 1/3rd of the marks allotted for the question will be deducted for each wrong answer).
*   **Unattempted Questions:** No marks are awarded or deducted for questions that are not attempted.

**Score Calculation Formula:**

Your Raw Score = (Number of Correct Answers × Marks per Correct Answer) - (Number of Incorrect Answers × Negative Marks per Incorrect Answer)

Candidates are advised to thoroughly review their response sheets and the provisional answer key, calculate their scores, and submit any valid objections within the stipulated timeframe to ensure a fair evaluation.

2. Look for the link 'UP Board High School (Class X) Examination - 2026 Results' or 'UP Board Intermediate (Class XII) Examination - 2026 Results'.
3. Click on the relevant link for your class (10th or 12th).
4. A new page will open where you need to enter your Roll Number as per your admit card.
5. Enter any other required details, such as your School Code or Captcha, if prompted.
6. Click on the 'Submit' or 'View Result' button.
7. Your UP Board Class 10th or 12th Result 2026 will be displayed on the screen.
8. Download and take a printout of your result for future reference.

content:
## UP Board Class 10th & 12th Result 2026: Check Your Scores

The Uttar Pradesh Madhyamik Shiksha Parishad (UPMSP) is the largest examination conducting body in the world, responsible for administering the Class 10th (High School) and Class 12th (Intermediate) examinations annually. These results are critical for students as they mark the culmination of their efforts and serve as a gateway to higher education and various career opportunities.

### Important Dates and Schedules

Students are advised to keep track of the following important dates related to the UP Board Class 10th & 12th Results 2026. While exact dates are subject to official announcement, a typical timeline is provided below:

| Event                                 | Tentative Date (2026)      |
| :------------------------------------ | :------------------------- |
| Class 10th & 12th Exams Conducted     | February - March           |
| Result Declaration Date               | April - May                |
| Scrutiny/Re-evaluation Application    | May - June                 |
| Compartment/Improvement Exam          | July - August              |
| Compartment/Improvement Result        | August - September         |

### How to Verify Your Result Details

Upon downloading your provisional mark sheet, it is crucial to verify all the details to ensure accuracy. In case of any discrepancy, students should immediately contact their respective schools or the UPMSP officials. Key details to check include:

*   **Candidate's Name:** Ensure your name is spelled correctly.
*   **Roll Number:** Match it with your admit card.
*   **Father's Name & Mother's Name:** Verify correct spellings.
*   **Date of Birth:** Confirm accuracy.
*   **Subject-wise Marks:** Check marks obtained in each subject.
*   **Total Marks:** Calculate and cross-verify the total.
*   **Grades/Division:** Confirm the assigned grade or division.
*   **Pass/Fail Status:** Ensure your overall result is correct.

### Mark Sheet and Certificates

While the online result is provisional, the original mark sheets and passing certificates will be issued by the UPMSP and distributed through the respective schools a few weeks after the online declaration. Students must collect their original documents from their schools as these are essential for further academic pursuits and professional endeavors.

### Post-Result Options

**For Class 10th Students:** Successful candidates can enroll in Class 11th in their chosen streams (Science, Commerce, Arts). Those unhappy with their marks can apply for re-evaluation or reappear in compartment exams if eligible.

**For Class 12th Students:** Qualified students can pursue higher education in various undergraduate courses, diploma programs, or competitive examinations. Similarly, options for re-evaluation and compartment exams will be available.

Stay updated with official announcements from UPMSP for the precise date and time of result declaration.

content:
## Latest Defence and Police Jobs 2026 Notification

The Ministry of Defence (MoD), Government of India, is actively recruiting for various posts across its esteemed organizations, including the Indian Army, Indian Air Force, Indian Navy, Border Security Force (BSF), Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF), DRDO, and State Police Departments. This recruitment drive offers more than 1600 promising career opportunities for eligible individuals with diverse educational backgrounds.

### Vacancy Details
Below is a list of some of the active recruitments under the Defence sector, along with their respective organizations, total vacancies, and closing dates:

| Post Name                      | Organization                     | Total Vacancies | Closing Date |
| :----------------------------- | :------------------------------- | :-------------- | :----------- |
| Non Executive (ITI)            | BEML Limited                     | 362             | 10/07/2026   |
| Apprentice Posts               | DRDO DESIDOC                     | 20              | 01/07/2026   |
| Agniveervayu                   | Indian Air Force                 | Not Specified   | 26/07/2026   |
| Group Y Airmen Medical Asst.   | Indian Air Force                 | Not Specified   | 26/07/2026   |
| SSC Officers                   | Indian Navy                      | 275             | 27/07/2026   |
| Group ‘C’ Posts                | Border Roads Organisation (BRO)  | 899             | 04/07/2026   |
| Paid Internship                | DEAL Dehradun                    | 45              | 30/06/2026   |
| Research Posts                 | INMAS DRDO                       | 15              | 30/06/2026   |
| Agniveer (Apprentice) Posts    | Indian Navy                      | Not Specified   | 29/06/2026   |

### Eligibility Criteria

#### Educational Qualifications
Candidates from various educational backgrounds are eligible to apply for these defence and police jobs. The required qualifications typically include:

*   **10th Pass:** For various Group D and entry-level positions.
*   **12th Pass (HSC/SSC):** For numerous clerical, technical, and other non-gazetted posts.
*   **ITI / NCVT Certificate Holders:** For technical trades and skilled worker roles.
*   **Diploma Holders:** For engineering and technical assistant positions.
*   **Graduates (Bachelor's Degree):** For officer-level, administrative, and specialized roles.

#### Age Limit
Age limits vary significantly depending on the specific post and the recruiting organization. Candidates are strongly advised to refer to the individual official notifications for the exact age criteria, including any relaxations applicable to reserved categories.

### Application Fee
Application fees for defence and police recruitments are specific to each notification and post. Candidates are advised to check the official notification of the desired post for details regarding application fees, payment modes, and any fee exemptions for reserved categories.

### Important Dates

| Event                      | Date           |
| :------------------------- | :------------- |
| Application Closing Date   | Up to 27/07/2026 |
| *Specific exam dates will be announced in official notifications.* |

### How to Apply

To apply for any of the mentioned Defence and Police job vacancies, follow these general steps:

1.  **Identify the specific post:** Review the list of available vacancies and choose the post(s) you are eligible and interested in.
2.  **Refer to Official Notification:** Download and thoroughly read the detailed official notification for the chosen post from the respective organization's official website. This document contains complete information on eligibility, age limits, application fees, selection process, and important dates.
3.  **Online Registration:** Visit the official recruitment portal of the concerned department/organization.
4.  **Fill Application Form:** Complete the online application form with all required personal, educational, and contact details accurately.
5.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, educational certificates, and any other necessary documents as per the specified format and size.
6.  **Pay Application Fee:** If an application fee is applicable, pay it through the available online payment modes (Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI).
7.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all entered details before final submission. Print a copy of the submitted application form for future reference.

### Relevant Top Government Job Pages

For more detailed and categorized job alerts, candidates can explore the following pages (Note: These are general category pages and not direct application links for the current vacancies. Always refer to official departmental websites for specific recruitment notifications):

*   [Officer Level Govt Jobs](https://www.example.com/officer-jobs-category)

*(Note: Always refer to the official websites of the respective recruiting organizations for the most accurate and up-to-date information regarding specific job alerts and application procedures.)*

content:
## RRB Technician Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 6557 Vacancies

The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has announced a forthcoming recruitment drive for Technician posts in 2026, signaling a significant opportunity for individuals with technical skills to join the prestigious Indian Railways. This notification anticipates approximately 6557 vacancies across various departments, offering a stable and rewarding career path. Candidates are encouraged to keep an eye on the official RRB websites for detailed information, application timelines, and examination procedures.

### Vacancy Details

A total of 6557 Technician posts are expected to be filled through this recruitment process. The exact breakdown of vacancies by trade and railway zone will be provided in the detailed official notification.

| Post Name   | Total Vacancies |
| :---------- | :-------------- |
| Technician  | 6557 (Approx.)  |

### Important Dates and Exam Schedule

Prospective applicants should note that the specific dates for application submission and examination are yet to be announced. Please refer to the official RRB notification for the most accurate and updated schedule.

| Event                          | Date                  |
| :----------------------------- | :-------------------- |
| Online Application Start Date  | To be announced (TBA) |
| Online Application End Date    | To be announced (TBA) |
| Last Date for Fee Payment      | To be announced (TBA) |
| CBT Exam Date                  | To be announced (TBA) |
| Admit Card Release             | To be announced (TBA) |
| Result Declaration             | To be announced (TBA) |

### Application Fee

Candidates will be required to pay an application fee as per the category. The fee can typically be paid online using Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or other specified payment modes.

| Category                                 | Application Fee |
| :--------------------------------------- | :-------------- |
| General / OBC                            | ₹500/-          |
| SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen / Female / EBC | ₹250/-          |

*Note: A portion of the fee for certain categories might be refunded upon appearing in the first stage CBT.*

### Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill the following eligibility criteria to apply for the RRB Technician posts:

#### Educational Qualification:
Applicants must possess:
*   Matriculation/SSLC along with an ITI certificate from NCVT/SCVT in relevant trades (e.g., Fitter, Electrician, Welder, Machinist, etc.) OR
*   A Diploma in Engineering in a relevant discipline OR
*   A Degree in Engineering in a relevant discipline (for certain higher technician roles).

*Specific trade-wise qualifications will be detailed in the full notification.*

#### Age Limit:
*   **Minimum Age:** 18 years
*   **Maximum Age:** 33 years

**Age Relaxation:**
*   **SC/ST:** 5 years
*   **OBC (Non-Creamy Layer):** 3 years
*   **PwBD (UR):** 10 years
*   **PwBD (OBC):** 13 years
*   **PwBD (SC/ST):** 15 years
*   Ex-Servicemen and other categories will receive age relaxation as per Government of India rules.

### Selection Process

The selection process for RRB Technician posts typically involves the following stages:
1.  **Computer Based Test (CBT):** Candidates will undergo an online objective multiple-choice examination.
2.  **Document Verification (DV):** Shortlisted candidates will be called for verification of their original documents.
3.  **Medical Examination:** Candidates must pass a prescribed medical fitness test conducted by the Railway Administration.

### How to Apply (Step-by-Step)

Interested and eligible candidates can apply for the RRB Technician Vacancy 2026 by following these steps once the official application window opens:

1.  **Visit Official RRB Websites:** Go to the official website of the Railway Recruitment Board from which you intend to apply.
2.  **New Registration:** Click on the 'New Registration' link for Technician posts (CEN).
3.  **Fill Application Form:** Provide all required personal, educational, and contact details accurately in the online application form.
4.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other necessary documents (e.g., educational certificates, caste certificate) as per the specified format and size.
5.  **Pay Application Fee:** Proceed to pay the application fee through the available online payment modes.
6.  **Review and Submit:** Carefully review all the entered details before final submission to avoid errors.
7.  **Print Acknowledgment:** After successful submission, download and print a copy of the completed application form for your records.

### Important Links

*   **Official RRB Websites:** Candidates are advised to regularly visit the official websites of the Railway Recruitment Boards (e.g., rrbcdg.gov.in, rrbajmer.gov.in, etc.) for the latest updates.
*   **Official Notification PDF:** To be released soon.
*   **Apply Online Link:** To be activated upon official notification release.

content:
### RPSC RAS Recruitment 2026: Apply for 607 Administrative Posts

The Rajasthan Public Service Commission (RPSC) has released a notification for the much-anticipated Rajasthan Administrative Service (RAS) Recruitment 2026. This recruitment drive aims to fill 607 prestigious positions across various administrative and allied services within the state of Rajasthan. Aspiring candidates are encouraged to prepare for this significant opportunity to contribute to the state's governance.

#### Vacancy Details

| Post Name                           | Total Vacancies |
| :---------------------------------- | :-------------- |
| Rajasthan Administrative Service (RAS) & Allied Services | 607             |

*Note: Detailed post-wise distribution of vacancies will be provided in the official notification.*

#### Important Dates

Candidates should regularly check the RPSC official website for the exact dates.

| Event                            | Date               |
| :------------------------------- | :----------------- |
| Notification Release Date        | To Be Announced    |
| Online Application Start Date    | To Be Announced    |
| Online Application End Date      | To Be Announced    |
| Application Fee Payment Last Date | To Be Announced    |
| Prelims Exam Date                | To Be Announced (2026) |
| Mains Exam Date                  | To Be Announced (2026) |
| Admit Card Release               | To Be Announced    |

#### Application Fee

Applicants will be required to pay a non-refundable application fee as per their category. The exact fee structure will be detailed in the official notification. (Based on typical RPSC fees)

| Category                     | Application Fee (Tentative) |
| :--------------------------- | :-------------------------- |
| General / OBC (Creamy Layer) | ₹450/-                      |
| OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) / EWS | ₹350/-                      |
| SC / ST / PWD / Sahariya     | ₹250/-                      |
| Correction Charge            | ₹500/-                      |

*Payment Mode: Online through Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking.*

#### Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible for the RPSC RAS Recruitment 2026, candidates must meet the following criteria:

**1. Educational Qualification:**
*   Candidates must possess a Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University established by law in India, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Government.
*   Candidates appearing in the final year of a degree course or who are awaiting their final year result shall also be eligible to apply, but they must produce proof of having passed the degree course before appearing for the Main Examination.

**2. Age Limit (as on the prescribed cut-off date):**
*   **Minimum Age:** 21 Years
*   **Maximum Age:** 40 Years (for RAS posts)
*   Age relaxation will be provided as per the Rajasthan Government rules for various reserved categories (e.g., SC/ST/OBC, Women, PWD, Ex-servicemen). Specific details will be in the official notification.

#### How to Apply for RPSC RAS Recruitment 2026

Interested and eligible candidates must apply online through the official website of the Rajasthan Public Service Commission (RPSC) once the application window opens. Follow these general steps:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official RPSC website (rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in) or the SSO Portal (sso.rajasthan.gov.in).
2.  **One Time Registration (OTR):** If you are a new user, complete your One Time Registration (OTR) on the SSO Portal. This involves providing personal details, contact information, and creating a login ID and password.
3.  **Login:** Use your OTR credentials to log in to the application portal.
4.  **Find the Advertisement:** Locate the "RAS Recruitment 2026" notification and click on the "Apply Online" link.
5.  **Fill Application Form:** Carefully fill in all the required details in the online application form, including personal information, educational qualifications, and other relevant data.
6.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph and signature as per the specifications mentioned in the official notification.
7.  **Pay Application Fee:** Pay the application fee online through the available payment gateways (Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking).
8.  **Review and Submit:** Review all the entered details before final submission. Ensure there are no errors.
9.  **Print Application:** After successful submission, take a printout of the filled application form and the e-receipt for future reference.

#### Important Links

*   **Official RPSC Website:** [https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in/](https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in/)
*   **Official Notification (Once Released):** Will be updated here.
*   **Apply Online (Once Activated):** Will be updated here.
